commit 4130e508264f1341c9788efb700148be344e3372
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2021-05-05 23:55:41 +0000

PerfectMongoose (PM) update (#2)

I've essentially rebased my earlier AdvCiv changes from PM 3.2 onto PM 3.3 (extracted from MongooseMod 4.2a) and, in the process, revised many of the AdvCiv changes. I've ended up replacing or reverting the bulk of the PM 3.3 additions. Perhaps the main novelty that I've kept is that PM 3.3 takes distances between continents into account when splitting the map into a New World and Old World for the "Old World Starts" option. PM 3.3 also makes several tweaks and bugfixes in the (start) tile evaluation functions -- but I'm mostly letting the DLL handle the starting positions, so these aren't significant improvements. However, reviewing the PM 3.3 changes was instructive for tackling some issues that I had been meaning to address for some time.

For river placement, I'm now combining the standard river generator (DLL) with the PM river generator. And I've tweaked the PM generator to discourage rivers in extreme latitudes. This seems to achieve a reasonable spread of rivers and overall frequency. (The PM generator, on its own, tends to concentrate rivers in the tropics while leaving swaths of temperate land mostly dry.)

When splitting up the map for the Old World Starts option, shallow-water connections and (as a coarse predictor of habitibility) latitude are now taken into account. Inspired by Toffer's C2C_World.py, the sea level now gets recalculated while trying to break a New World continent off of a Pangaea. Through these and some smaller changes, the Old World Starts option now seems to succeed somewhat reliably (at least more often than not?) at reserving a sizable New World, and no longer leads to an unpredictable land-sea ratio. AdvCiv's starting position algorithm [advc.027] now handles the assignment of starting sites even when playing with Old World Starts: PM reserves New World continents, and the DLL does all the rest based on that constraint.

Fixed some problems at the map boundaries, in particular hills and peaks caused by "attenuation" near the poles. And tweaked the slope-based method for placing peaks and hills so as to discourage large clumps, coastal elevations and straight, uninterrupted mountain chains.

Snow hills can again - rarely - occur in temperate latitudes to represent alpine areas. Apart from that, Tundra and Snow are confined to their appropriate latitudes, with more varied boundary regions than in AdvCiv 0.99. (Although the boundaries are sometimes still straighter than I'd like.) In part, this has been accomplished through a change adopted from C2C_World.py that adjusts per-tile temperature values to the tile's distance from the sea. There is now also a timberline running through the Tundra.

The PerfectWorld 2 landmass generator is available as an option (as it was in the original script; AdvCiv had removed that option along with several others). Seems to work well enough but, unfortunately, isn't much better at generating massive continents than the (default) Perlin noise generator. Both generators can produce large continents, but usually in a snaky shape that impedes interactions (warfare) between the civs. This remains an open issue; I don't think tweaks to the existing parameters can resolve this.

I've held off on the removal of map options and on changes specific to the AdvCiv DLL until the last few commits in the pull request. This should make it easy to obtain a version of the script that is portable to other mods.
commit f8fc332f4c60032947f1336b9b28bb1095951456
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2021-04-14 23:17:22 +0200

Rewrite output of combat modifiers in combat odds help

So that modifiers with an unexpected sign (e.g. -25% city attack) are handled correctly, i.e. shown with the correct sign and in the correct color. In AdvCiv, this had been a problem only for the Disorganized promotion (from K-Mod), but it's nice, for mod-mods, to have a flexible program logic. I've also eliminated redundancies, making the code easier to extend.

AI: cancel GPT deals when per-player limit exceeded

[change id advc.133]

To address the gold subsidy loophole:
https://forums.civfanatics.com/threads/subsidies-and-aggressive-trading-practices.173468/

Not a big issue in AdvCiv because the AI can evaluate resources reasonably well through [advc.036], but (per-rival) GPT limits still exist and shouldn't be circumvented.

Tweak UWAI prediction of siege outcomes

Estimate the defensive strength of garrisons more highly. This is supposed to account for the advantage that (medium-size and large) defensive stacks tend to gain from choosing the best defender against a given attacker.

commit 4ae382e5dbec429eddcc84e08935e3f94d01a20a
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2021-03-20 23:37:23 +0100

Revised all code in the UWAI component

... and also AdvCiv4lerts.

I've tested this a bit; looks like there can't be many new bugs left. Will have to do a side-by-side review of the changes later (and do a lot more testing); for now I just want to have it committed.

The style now conforms with the style that I use in the rest of the GameCore DLL. Floating-point numbers been replaced almost entirely with fixed-point numbers (ScaledNum), in the UWAI component and in all other synchronized code, the exception being a couple of uses of std::log and the (BtS/ Lead From Behind) combat odds calculations. if nothing else, this seems to have improved performance - though other factors could be responsible for that as well. A lot of low-level refactoring, some bugfixes; the important ones, I'll commit separately on another branch.

commit 9640ae18cf98e2ca295ff3d04c55065e4455a795

Revise and reorganize the Actual Quotes mod a bit

Actual Quotes was based on full copies of the diplomacy text files from Vanilla, Warlords and the BtS expansion. I've kept those separate files but deleted all texts that the mod doesn't actually modify. And I've added brief comments that describe any changes that don't deal with leader-specific texts.

I've also restored a few lighthearted texts that I kind of like and that the mod had replaced, and I've added a couple of leader-specific texts for declarations of war -- as a starting point. (Neither the original game nor Actual Quotes has such texts, but I think the engine supports them.) [advc.005f]

Clean up string-based lookup of global enum ids

Now that this is properly resolved (see the last, recently edited paragraph), CvGlobals will lookup both info and non-info ids only when explicitly prompted by CvXMLLoadUtility::getGlobalEnumFromString (new function).

commit ccefc3a0497ab847cb6bbe5ae7b4692d47301646
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2021-02-16 20:58:29 +0100

Fix problems with improvement upgrade rate

Fixed a K-Mod bug that had caused the AI to value cottages /less/ highly when in Emancipation. (At least I'm pretty sure that this was wrong. Let's hope.) [advc.001]

Count the improvement upgrade rate at times-100 precision so that the Emancipation ability works as expected for values that aren't a multiple of 100 and for negative values (so long as they're not less than -100, i.e. still can't let Hamlets decay back to Cottages). [advc.912f]

To make this work, the number of turns that an improvement has been worked is now also counted at times-100 precision. This requires an int counter instead of a short int, but I've kept the size of CvPlot (kind of important for cache performance) the same as before by replacing another int with a char.

commit e8bfef1ca5cd2c188c2ad01365760ed750a7b3bc
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2021-02-16 19:57:54 +0100

Refactor combat odds calc, damage limit bugfix

Move the getCombatOdds and LFBgetCombatOdds functions into a new translation unit, split them up so that they can share some code with each other and so that the Advanced Combat Odds mod (ACO) can also reuse some of it. (To be done in another commit: Throw out the code now in calculateCombatOddsLegacy, move the ACO code.) Refactored all the odds-related code, but mostly just the variable names (which had made the ACO code quite difficult to comprehend).

Primarily, this should make it more feasible for mod-mods to change the combat rules. (Updating the odds calculations is a real obstacle to that.)

Added code for verifying the calculated odds through simulations of the combat resolution procedure. Found one bug this way, which had already been fixed in ACO (i.e. the displayed odds were wrong only when ACO was disabled): The BtS/ Lead From Behind odds had assumed that combat ends when the attacker's damage limit is reached, but combat resolution actually requires the limit to be exceeded. This matters for example when a Cannon attacks another Cannon because the damage limit (80) is then a multiple of the per-round damage.

I've chosen to fix the bug by changing the combat resolution procedure -- combat now ends upon reaching the combat limit. I've updated the ACO code accordingly. The hit that reaches the damage limit is now also included in the Combat Log. [advc.001l]

Disable RMB back-cycling via worker cycling button

[change id advc.154]

commit 3354ca48c50b70a95c14d367f04833964546052b

Date:   2021-01-31 04:50:01 +0100

Minor misc. refactoring

commit 8b3371a65e89457a745d66e7bc1e226e1759ebbe
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2021-01-31 04:39:59 +0100

Stricter conditions for AI opening borders for naval trade

A revealed land tile now only makes an AI civ willing to open its borders if the tile is on the same landmass as a city owned by the AI civ. If it's on a different landmass, then there needs to be a trade connection (via Sailing or Astronomy). The AI will also open its borders if it has a unit adjacent to a tile that it can't currently enter but will be able to enter through an OB agreement. (And, as before, the AI is willing to open its borders regardless of accessibility if its attitude level is one above the threshold set in XML.) [advc.124]

commit f849392ff4bf6c12df0d8e609ca0946e5206618d
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2021-01-13 21:26:33 +0100

UWAI: Fix some issues with AI capitulation decisions

These changes should align the "not right now" response (computed quickly on the human turn by CvTeamAI) better with the decisions made by the UWAI component (on the AI turn).

And, in situations when an AI civ is fighting multiple wars, the all-war-ending effect of a capitulation is now taken into account. (Not reliably, but I hope it'll work well enough most of the time.)

Also fixes a flipped sign (wasn't terribly consequential though).

To address problems apparent from the savegames posted here:
https://forums.civfanatics.com/threads/quick-advciv-questions-answers.666137/#post-15997844

commit 074c73cd051b8e3c683b02447b1d983a66856b9f

Date:   2021-01-13 21:20:49 +0100

UWAI: Tweak "public opposition" aspect of war utility

I think this had too much impact when war weariness got really bad. Also reduced the impact when close to a military victory. This was one of several problems in the savegames posted here:
https://forums.civfanatics.com/threads/quick-advciv-questions-answers.666137/#post-15997844

commit 68196b1cd152615955a0cbece59f8987acdfc5d6
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2021-01-13 21:14:37 +0100

UWAI: Tweak "loathing" aspect of war utility calculation

Disregard the loathing aspect when expecting to be eliminated soon. This should prevent the AI from continuing suicidal wars just to inconvenience an enemy.

When computing war utility from the point of view of a human civ (relevant e.g. for peace terms), don't completely disregard the loathing aspect, i.e. assume that human players are to an extent vengeful.

Fix AI not respecting AdvCiv rule about tech requirements

For example, the Earth1000AD scenario lets India start with Paper but not with any of the prerequisite techs of Paper. In this case, India should not be allowed to research Education (which requires Paper) until India has discovered the requirements for Paper. The AI had been ignoring that rule (since the update to K-Mod 1.46 I think). [advc.126]

BUG option for disabling savemap key combination

commit 529ebb7b4f59681b609c10c4d3ad31838ba62d67
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-12-17 23:35:17 +0100

Improve AI support for mods with custom eras

Some new tags in Civ4EraInfos.xml through which mods can tell the AI e.g. in which era (if any) oceangoing ships become available. And a tag that allows mods to map any custom eras to the BtS era numbers (0 to 6) so that the AI is aware that e.g. era #1 is in the midgame when a mod has only 3 eras. [advc.erai]

Inspired by this Kek-Mod commit:
DarkLunaPhantom/Civ4-K-Mod@8b9aecdd4cfabb393d0bba3f3ed77cb2cd78a680
(I've directly adopted one small AI change concerning the counting of religions, under change id [kekm.36].)

Also added a tag that enables all techs of an era as goody hut outcomes, thus making part of change [advc.314] configurable through XML.

And a bunch of minor refactoring changes. (I've gone through all instances of the word "Era" in the AI code, so a lot of style irregularities have caught my eye.)

commit 9a623ad895c73c2ce0fe4d5f5cf668ab04848307
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-12-12 15:43:15 +0100

Let SPI handle team games, plus other changes to starting sites

Greedy algorithm for assigning starting sites in team games added to the StartingPositionIteration (SPI) class. The BtS algorithm is still used for improving on the greedy solution. Also refactored the BtS algorithm (superficially). SPI still isn't used for team games with a high player count and high average team size - because the greedy algorithm doesn't handle that well (and the BtS algorithm does OK). [advc.027]

The BtS algorithm for swapping around starting sites in team games is now executed right after the initial assignment of starting sites, i.e. it's no longer part of the normalization step. [advc.108b]

Starting site handicaps from the difficulty setting (StartingLocPercent) now also apply to team games. This is implemented by applying the average handicap of each team to all its members. [advc.108b]

SPI now respects tiles marked as "StartingPlot" in scenarios that allow arbitrary civ leaders (e.g. Africa). SPI only finds additional starting sites when there are too few pre-marked sites. (That can easily happen; e.g. the base-game version of Europe has only 6 pre-marked starting sites.) [advc.027]

SPI now handles starting sites for players with the "RandomStartLocation" attribute set in a scenario file; except for scenarios with fixed civ leaders (CvInitCore::getWBMapNoPlayers()==false), which SPI still doesn't interfere with. [advc.027]

Dialed down the randomization of found-city values when picking a "RandomStartLocation". [advc.027]

commit 96c6996a71eeef103f2a7895347afad913a09d53
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-11-17 00:15:16 +0100

Refactor AI strength memory

[change id advc.158]

Moved into a separate class. Enabled for humans (and minor civs) because I think the only potential OOS issues with humans are in Debug-mode help text (fixed by this commit; change advc.001n) and in the cost function of the pathfinder (already addressed by karadoc). I'll still have to test it in multiplayer though. Strength memory disabled for Barbarians because I don't think Barbarians need to be able to bypass strongly defended tiles (and I've other plans for Barbarian strength memory).

Now uses a hash map, which was slightly faster than a vector in the test I've run.

commit 95e434343b1242b86138b89ac3a64de29d194160
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-11-12 02:02:36 +0100

Revise AI_targetCityVal

... i.e. how the AI decides which enemy city to target. Now based on UWAI data (specifically: path distances, AI_assetVal). I've kept most of the old code as a fallback because the UWAI cache isn't always up to date (and for the K-Mod/Legacy AI option). [advc.104d]

Unless the attacker is very powerful militarily, AI_targetCityVal now prefers cities with low tile defense and (for naval assaults) continents with few enemy cities.

Added some ad-hoc code for targeting the enemy's strategic resources. (A proper evaluation of a resource from the enemy's point of view - and whether conquering the city will flip the resource tile - would be too much work.)

Per-area target cities are now updated when AI war plans change.

Reduced the value assigned to Shrines in AI_assetVal. This is relevant for UWAI, AI_targetCityVal and AI_citTradeVal. The K-Mod evaluation is sensible, but I don't think it was on the right scale.

The Barbarians no longer pick a permanent (per-continent) target city. [advc.300]

The AI can no longer pick target cities that are neither revealed nor deducible. This visibility cheat was probably inadvertent. [advc.001]

TeamPathFinder can now reuse allocated memory even if the team (for which paths are generated) changes. I'm not making use of this after all though because resetting the node data in between pathfinder calls is still a bit too slow, and pathfinding in AI_targetCityVal isn't really needed; the distance information from the UWAI cache suffices. [advc.pf]

Resolve a minor open issue with AI naval reinforcements

When deciding whether to reinforce a naval assault against a city tile (i.e. an amphibious landing), BtS had disregarded stacks with any siege units (which can't attack amphibiously). commit 9661bf97df10247b2903d767ba3d4e0f8791b943
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-11-03 23:10:10 +0100

Ignore human vassal status when show Demographics

Normally, the (revised) Demographics tab is supposed to show only data about "rivals", and civs in a vassal-master (or sibling vassal) relationship aren't considered rivals. But human vassal will probably want to break free at some point, so it's more helpful to treat everyone as a rival (except teammates). [advc.077]

commit 4ba820eb7673d8c1319080d5c9b6e760cfe006d6
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-11-03 23:02:43 +0100

Fix issue with action recommendations at game start

Plot colors were being updated before player options (in particular "No Action Recommendations") were set. This had caused blue circles to be shown on goody huts regardless of options. That's a BtS bug. The blue circles had also prevented AdvCiv from showing resource-layer bubbles for goody huts. [advc.004z]

Similar problem: The initial autosave gets created before Python reports the active Globe layer to the DLL. Therefore, the active layer stored in an initial autosave needs to be ignored in order to apply the "Start with Resource Icons" option correctly. [advc.004m]

commit fc7640933b544f0f943f527e2edc08999da1dc00
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-10-25 23:36:14 +0100

Let sea and air units enter rival forts through OB

In order to make forts more similar to cities, streamlining the rules. Also addressed a minor oddity with unowned forts: They had been granting +25% tile defense but didn't enable city defense modifiers (e.g. Garrison promotion). Now all defensive advantages apply to unowned forts. [advc.183]

In the process, I've replaced the CvPlot member functions isCity(bool,TeamTypes), isFriendlyCity, isEnemyCity, isValidDomainForLocation, isValidDomainForAction with CvTeam and CvUnit member functions. The new functions fix some minor (BtS) information leaks by distinguishing between revealed and unrevealed improvement type. Change ids advc.124, advc.299 are affected by these refactoring changes.

The Fortify action is no longer recommended for foreign cities. (Those recommendations were unwise and had leaked information.) [advc.004]

Also slightly refactored and tweaked the AI checks for ships (in a city or fort) being threatened from land. [advc.139]

commit 96989508e32b5f5ada636353db26de31f4b0a7f4
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-10-22 00:59:55 +0200

Cache isthmuses, total number of plots

[change id advc.opt]

The presence of isthmuses blocking sea movement is the only check that the pathfinders have to make on a per-edge basis (as opposed to per node). Need only 1 bit to pre-compute and store whether there is any isthmus adjacent to a water plot. (For the vast majority of water plots, there is none.)

The total number of plots was constantly being re-computed by CvMap as grid width times grid height.

Refactoring in path finding code (commit 2/2)

[change id advc.pf]

- New header and implementation file FAStarFunc.h/cpp for functions removed from CvGameCoreUtils.

- Initialize static K-Mod path finder (CvSelectionGroup) only once the map dimensions are known.

commit 60a15e6434fe8379a8a642055dff0f34238bb50b
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-09-20 14:41:55 +0200

Handle assignment of random civs and leaders in the DLL

That is, keep track of external calls to CvRandom to determine which players receive a random civ or leader from the EXE. Display that information on the Settings tab, and use it to avoid giving away the – randomly chosen – civs and leaders of human rivals via the scoreboard in network games. [advc.190c]

(Some other, older additions to the Settings tab are now tagged with advc.190a, 190b instead of 004.)

Overwrite the random choices of the EXE so that civs with multiple leaders have a higher probability of being selected. (But not high enough to give every leader equal probability on the bottom line; Mansa Musa will still be chosen more often than Roosevelt.) [advc.191]

commit 6102c9181ff39e45daffa060675b89dc21c5d69c
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-07-19 03:53:15 +0200

Prefer starting sites with a resource just out of reach

Only when StartingPositionIteration (SPI) is enabled. Increase the found-city value a bit when there is a resource or other high-yield tile just outside the city radius. (This doesn't by any means guarantee that every player will find such a resource near their starting site.) In order to make the decision whether to move the starting settler more interesting. It's the first decision in the game and SPI had made settling in place optimal most of the time. [advc.027]

commit f429f6e13054084feaa84c996cc891e55abc6243
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-07-19 04:02:33 +0200

Fix AdvCiv bug: unique range of resources ignored

Introduced in AdvCiv 0.97. There are two iUnique tags that are supposed to enforce a minimum distance in between resources. One for resource classes (e.g. Livestock), one for resources (e.g. Cow). The latter had been ignored. The most easily observable effect was that Fish resources (they're especially common) had appeared next to each other; they're supposed to be 2 tiles apart. For strategic resources, there was still the unique range from the bonus class to fall back on.

Bug report (I guess):
https://forums.civfanatics.com/goto/post?id=15829227#post-15829227

commit 68ebb86656a7f84772b499520e517039341940ce
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-07-19 03:35:24 +0200

Randomize tile for normalizeAddFood; prefer inner ring.

[change id advc.108]

The tile for food resources placed during normalization was, shockingly, not randomized. The choice of the food resource wasn't randomized either, but that has already been changed in AdvCiv 0.97. When possible, BtS had just placed Corn east of the starting site.

normalizeAddExtras had already been choosing random tiles. Both functions now have a bias for the inner ring. Because this gives players more leeway to move the starting settler.

commit 2ff9f8bbb38b68c07676249432881f55ad2fb753
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-07-19 04:28:31 +0200

Tweak placement of area-unique resources

Now there is a preference for areas with a high number of tiles where the resource can be placed, e.g. with a lot of flat Jungle in the case of Banana. Previously, it was only based on the total tile count. [advc.129]

commit 568291788d62587233bad248c274338c276aba6f
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-07-19 03:05:48 +0200

Increase starting lake chance again (a little)

Because I've realized that a short river makes it difficult to move the starting settler without losing freshwater access. A lake provides freshwater to up to 8 tiles. [advc.108]

commit 48e93752d05a44c167cc994101bc2e6cc8a2caa0
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-07-19 02:58:53 +0200

Don't place unreachable water resources

Water tiles that could only be worked from a city on an impassable tile (Peak) are counted as unworkable, meaning that they have 0 yield and no resources can be placed there. [advc.129d]

Not covered: Water tiles workable by a city - but fully enclosed by Ice and thus not reachable for Work Boats. Such tiles shouldn't receive resources, but that's not so easy to prevent.

commit 86cc0d11ab0fd8ede90cd81957f7a69a988661e5
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-07-16 22:45:31 +0200

Let friendly Fort w/o route connect water tiles

[change id advc.124]

Tweak (bugfix?) for the trade network rules. As shown in this screenshot by CFC user crullerdonut
https://forums.civfanatics.com/attachments/no-trade-routes-allowed-jpg.562884/
a Fort without a route did not connect water tiles. The screenshot is from AdvCiv, but I think it works the same way in BtS.

That's counterintuitive; ships don't travel on roads and railroads. A route is still required in order to connect a land resource on the Fort tile.

commit e4e936ce468c2f548325e50ac01f2d45f3463c76
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-07-16 22:31:59 +0200

Show map rand, sync rand seed in Debug mode

[change id advc.027b]

That is, store the initial seed, and show it in Debug mode if Ctrl is held down when hovering over the big flag on the main screen. So that map generation can be reproduced even when no initial seeds were set in CivilizationIV.ini -- by reading the randomly generated seeds off the flag button and entering them in CivilizationIV.ini. This could become helpful for dealing with user feedback on the starting position iteration algorithm.

Changed the normalization code so that only map rand is used; in BtS, it's a mix of map rand and sync rand, and that gets in the way of reproducing regenerated maps.

And a small tweak to [advc.108]: Jungle on a resource in the ring of tiles surrounding a starting city radius is now cleared with a probability of 1/2 instead of 2/3.

commit cb93c6bbae49b5b8ce6c4432d21d6e25a72fbba6
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-06-28 21:59:12 +0200

Tweak lake placement near starting sites

[change id advc.108]

When adding a lake for freshwater during the normalization step, tiles adjacent to the starting site are now (strictly) preferred. BtS had also done that, but K-Mod had randomized the placement of lakes (without regard for the distance from the starting site; probably an oversight by karadoc).

Since the AI knows how to move its starting settler, no freshwater source gets added during normalization if there is already a freshwater tile adjacent to the starting site (i.e. a freshwater source two tiles away from the starting site).
This change can be disabled through NORMALIZE_STARTPLOTS_AGGRESSIVELY=1 in GlobalDefines_advc.xml.
Note: Even if a player chooses not to move the settler onto a freshwater tile, adjacent freshwater can still be useful for irrigation.

When the game decides whether a starting site needs extra food, Whale is no longer counted as an available food source (unless starting in Renaissance or later). [advc.001]

commit 82c08218c66b1a01049f4bf81647a77ab3949573
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-06-28 21:01:25 +0200

More tweaks, fixed miscalculations for starting positions

I've now seen decent results for a variety of single-player maps. The new algorithm (starting position iteration, SPI) is now enabled by default -- although more testing is needed.

Made not_too_Big_or_Small maps smaller again because SPI is now more willing to let civs start on the small landmasses. [advc.165]

Slightly increased the land-to-sea ratio of PerfectMongoose because, even without the Old World Starts option, there is typically a fair amount of land that civs can't access at game start and the landmasses where civs do start were a bit too crowded; SPI didn't handle it well. Also decreased the recommended player count (shown on the Custom Game screen) for the Old World Starts option because PerfectMongoose usually fails to reserve a large continent for the New World. (That's a problem for another day; changing the recommendation is only a band-aid.) [advc.021b]

When re-assigning starting sites based on handicap, the game now tries to avoid giving human players a site for which SPI has computed a high "volatility" value. Being very close to another site, being isolated and reliance on early colonization contribute to the volatility of a site. (The re-assignment algorithm doesn't go out of its way far here, so volatile human starts are still very much a possibility.)

commit d6ea7fc8701bfe2d4353437da01e5d5b392d8b7e
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-06-21 23:14:06 +0200

Starting positions (cont.), normalization.

Completed the starting position iteration (SPI) algorithm (yet to be documented in the manual), but still need to test it on various maps and probably make adjustments based on those tests. For now SPI is disabled by default through GlobalDefines_advc.xml. Log output is disabled through the DLL (StartingPositionsIteration.cpp). [advc.027]

Modified PerfectMongoose so that SPI can work on it. [advc.027]

Made Not Too Big Or Small a little larger again (partly reverting [advc.165]) because it seems that SPI will usually place most civs on the "Big" landmass. (I'm still trying to figure out if that's really the most balanced way to set that map up.)

Starting site "normalization" is now done based on byproducts of SPI (if it is enabled). Specifically, the SPI data fully determines the placement of extra resources and features (forest, oasis, flood plains) and has some (small) impact on the placement of additional food near sites where food isn't already abundant. The reassignment of starting sites based on difficulty (StartingLocPercent) also uses SPI data. [advc.027]

Made some other functional and refactoring changes to normalization: [advc.108]
- Far fewer extra forests are placed. BtS basically places forests everywhere when a site's found value is too low. That looks conspicuous and doesn't help much. Greater care is also taken not to place too many flood plains.
- When placing extra resources, resource types that already exist in the city radius are probabilistically avoided, especially in tiles adjacent to the existing resource, and especially for resources that don't normally form groups.
- Avoid placing food as extra resources when a site already has enough food and just needs to be buffed.
- Coastal seafood resources now count as "high-food" resources when deciding whether a starting site has enough food.
- Normalization may add at most 2 hills (up to a total of 3 as in BtS).
- Additional (non-water) food and hills have to be placed on the same landmass as the capital (from PerfectMongoose/ PerfectWorld2); other extra resources also aren't placed on other landmasses - if it can be avoided.
- Gold can no longer be placed as an extra resource.

The normalization step tries to avoid placing resources on goody huts; removes the hut if it can't be avoided. (Stacking resources with huts isn't good because the resource layer also shows hut icons in AdvCiv.) [advc.004z]

commit e9a58d296e3eb725d2d97be6d6eb5b1c2a1e6646

[change id advc.131]

commit 96d6710603295e681a1ee208c101a5d978df1d74
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-05-29 17:39:50 +0200

Improve how AI deals with units that can't enter all terrain

Fix a BtS bug in the evaluation of Astronomy; no utility had been counted for the ability of Galleons to enter ocean. [advc.001]

The rest [advc.057] should only be relevant for mod-mods. Addresses problems reported here:
https://forums.civfanatics.com/threads/advanced-civ.614217/page-35#post-15775795

- Instead of just counting the impassable terrain and feature types, a fingerprint is computed (so that the AI can tell that e.g. a unit unable to enter Desert isn't necessarily compatible with one unable to enter Snow). An 3-bit impassable count is prepended to the fingerprint - to allow ordering units by their impassable counts.

- The AI ensures that no unit in a group has a higher impassable count than the unit leading the group. Meaning that leading units are always the least flexible unit in a group wrt. movement restrictions. This is accomplished by disallowing units that would violate the invariant stated above from joining.

- When a human civ comes under AI control (e.g. AI Auto Play), groups that don't satisfy the invariant are split up. AI code for handling human-AI status changes is now also called by the Change Player component (Alt+Z) [advc.127c] and the Rise & Fall component [advc.700]. Meaning that groups are split up if necessary and the AI attitude cache is updated. This was previously not being done after Alt+Z and had lead to failed assertions.

commit 0a47c0f4d68bc1a1b88b50fb0588b59de03a963c
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-05-29 17:00:48 +0200

UWAI: Better handling of per-instance unit cost modifier

For mod-mods; AdvCiv itself doesn't (yet) use the InstanceCostModifier tag. Fixing the problem reported here:
https://forums.civfanatics.com/threads/advanced-civ.614217/page-35#post-15774604

commit a70e4ce3a1db01c51da7fa370a706d8784191b38
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-05-28 00:44:41 +0200

Algorithm for fairer starting positions (wip)

Looks like I've committed a few changes to CvGame already in some earlier commit. Oh well. The whole shebang is for now disabled through CvGame::assignStartingPlots. (And even if it were enabled, the only effect would be a second or two of pointless computations.)

Implemented in StartingPositionIteration.cpp. It's still somewhat unclear how the iteration part will work.

Integration: The BtS starting position will be used as the initial solution of the iterative algorithm. Map scripts that override assignStartingPlots or findStartingPlot take precedence, i.e. the new code gets disabled then. I've made an exception for Pangaea, which really doesn't need custom code for its starting positions. If a script overrides findStartingArea, then my algorithm will restrict itself to sites on landmasses where BtS has placed starting sites.

The preliminaries (computed only once upfront) are done:

a) Some ten potential starting sites per civ that the main algorithm will choose from - computed through a mix of maximizing found-city values and "clearing the neighborhood".

b) Function evaluateWorkablePlot added to CitySiteEvaluator for evaluating individual plots as opposed to all plots around a city site. The new function uses mostly subroutines of my AIFoundValue class. I've refactored the code a bit to avoid duplication. Also some tweaks:
- Financial trait is now disregarded (like all other traits) when evaluating starting sites. [advc.031]
- Fix a bug in [advc.031] - multiple resources of the same (previously unavailable) type in the city radius had not been recognized as surplus resources.
- Use a flatter distribution of per-tile weights when placing a capital - because capitals tend to grow large.
- Starting sites with a high number of bad tiles are more strongly discouraged. [advc.108]

c) Table with path distances between all potential sites and all non-negligible workable plots (~1000 on a Standard-size map). Computed through Dijkstra's algorithm with a distance metric that allows steps from land to coastal water and from coastal water to ocean, but makes those moves (very) expensive.

Tbd.: a), b) and c) will be combined into a "start value" for each civ and, from the distribution of those start values, a fairness value will be computed. That's what the main algorithm is supposed to optimize, and the fairness value should also affect the "normalization" step in the end.

Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-05-27 23:53:35 +0200

Some misc. refactoring

commit 2a15829d174d621f75f3de2ea904e303fe0cb782
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-05-27 22:16:40 +0200

Add enum type for plot ids

[advc.enum]

To be done: An iterator for traversing all map plots. One can do this now:
FOR_EACH_ENUM(PlotNum)
{
   CvPlot const& p = GC.getMap().plotNum(eLoopPlotNum);
   // ...
}
But that's still pretty awkward. I've mainly added PlotNumTypes for EnumMap. Internally, EnumMap will use short int for PlotNumTypes. This means, future code will break when a map has more than 32768 tiles. I'm not aware of a playable scenario with that many tiles, but the "Giant" Earth scenarios come kind of close. If MAX_CIV_PLAYERS has been increased beyond 18, EnumMap uses __int32 for PlotNumTypes. Let's hope that no one will try to play a 200x200 map with just 18 civs.

commit b9646b64afc05424d404dd609eac1441eee93b0e
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-05-27 22:05:51 +0200

Fix AdvCiv bug: Barbarian Galley stuck in a loop

When Barbarians had appeared aboard a Galley (through [advc.306]), and that Galley was unable to reach any city because of ice and tried to drop its cargo at the nearest land tile, the Galley got stuck in a loop if the nearest land tile happened to be a peak. Looks like this only caused the Galley to miss one turn. It did trigger an assertion though.

commit 6e646b6312cb7210bf547e7808636bff0b4e70a8
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-05-27 21:51:03 +0200

Address issues with 2nd AI settler waiting for escort

Amends minor issues introduced with [advc.052] (which allows the AI, under certain conditions, to produce a 2nd settler before growing its capital to size 3):
* AI avoids producing its 2nd settler until it has a sufficient number of defenders in the capital (typically 2).
* So long as the AI hasn't met a human player, it may leave a single defender in its capital while using the other defender as an escort for the 2nd settler.

commit bf89749e67a248c6931b52ced53d3a042eeb2c1f
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-05-27 19:59:56 +0200

Separate flag for normalization in CitySiteEvaluator

When placing starting sites and when deciding how to normalize starting sites, found-city values need to be computed with some special provisions; not quite the same in both cases. BtS had a flag 'bStartingLoc' and normalization was determined based on that and by checking if starting locations had already been assigned. Meaning that, by default, the bStartingLoc behavior had applied also when normalizing. [advc.031e]
(I think it's better to be explicit about this and I may want to be able to compute found values as if normalizing as part of a fairer algorithm for placing starting sites. [advc.027])

Goody huts in the starting city radius are now counted for normalization. (It's a start ...) [advc.031]

[advc.xmldefault]

MEMORY_REJECTED_DEMAND and MEMORY_ACCEPT_DEMAND taken times 60%,
MEMORY_GIVE_HELP times 75% and
MEMORY_TRADED_TECH_TO_US times 25%.

commit 6673c5f7b2747b526ae282977e356ecd260b7ad7

Improve performance of trade connection check

By reordering the conditions in CvPlot::isTradeNetworkConnected and caching CvPlot::isImpassable.

Some 6-7% speedup. isTradeNetworkConnected had already been almost this fast until SHA 4fbc37dc0195399cf4ce7a0b56ea744384070e5d -- i.e. the bug fixed by that commit had saved time. So now it's fast AND correct (as best I can tell).

Tweak BUG Wonders tab (Debug mode, column width)

commit f086293436683a67c24ca769372d8bbbb8593503
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-04-24 21:59:44 +0200

Clean up "concealed player identity" mess

[change id advc.058]

K-Mod had changed CvPlayer::getName, getPlayerColor and the two getCivDescription functions so that they return "unknown" or the Barbarian color for players whom the active (human) player hasn't met or seen yet. That has been leading to some problems now and then, and it turns out that it's not a workable approach at all for the Rise & Fall component, which collects interface messages sent to AI civs in case that the human player will later take control of those civs. The player names and colors in those messages mustn't be based on the active player. Bug report:
https://forums.civfanatics.com/threads/advanced-civ.614217/page-32#post-15739533

Now the true names and colors are returned again by default, and there are separate functions getKnownName, getKnownPlayerColor etc. for the few situations in which identities may have to be concealed. Hopefully, I've correctly identified those situations.

And refactored CvPlayerColorInfo so that it uses ColorTypes instead of int.

Bugfix in AI proposals for dual deals

When asked to make a deal work that has 0 trade value for both sides, the AI should say that the deal is fine as it is. It had been saying that no deal is possible. In particular, the AI sometimes wouldn't propose a square peace treaty.

The bug was introduced in some recent commit.

Thanks to SuperXANA reporting the issue and providing a savegame.

Author: f1rpo <hidden>

Clear river ids at game start, store as short int.

commit 9112870f6f8e9674805169ecfb344225d58c0dd6
Author: f1rpo <hidden>

Tweak (increase) AI trade memory about city trades

commit 91ebe9eaa0fac82efca5ba70c7dfb8b968c51be8
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-04-21 23:35:28 +0200

Tweak revolt chance penalties, nationality bar help

[change id advc.101]

Indicate hurry anger, draft anger, foreign religion (if not being countered by owner's state religion) in the hover text of the nationality bar. The text doesn't say by how much these factors increase foreign culture strength; too complicated to implement.

Decrease the impact of hurry and draft anger. Previously, a city was treated as having 5 extra population while angry. Now it's 1.5 extra population per 1 unhappiness (from hurrying or drafting). And the 50% overall increase in foreign culture strength when angry is now adjusted to game speed (because the anger duration is longer in slower games) and taken times the square root of the unhappiness.

Reduced the impact of religion in cases when neither the city owner nor the cultural owner have a state religion or when neither of those religions is present in the city.

Instead of player tech era or game era, revolt chance now scales with a value computed from the percentage of techs discovered by cultural owner and city owner. That value gets quantized in order to avoid frequent changes in revolt probability.

Related discussion on CFC:
https://forums.civfanatics.com/threads/advanced-civ.614217/page-31#post-15735516

karadoc/Civ4-K-Mod@8c6e324ab4e02e1276b0f565d8746696d4f2ee51

Obviously by accident. Still, for reference, here's a CFC thread confirming that angry citizens indeed aren't supposed to consume food when producing a Settler or Worker:

commit 67496f6b3173ad5ab1938952124eedf450f841d1
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-04-13 21:00:10 +0200

Remove CvGlobals::m_aInfoVectors

That was a vector containing (pointers to) all vectors of Cv...Info records. Filling that vector of vectors had involved an unsafe cast and BtS had only needed it for a function CvGlobals::infosReset that the EXE might never even call. In case that the EXE does call it, I've rewritten infosReset using the DO_FOR_EACH_INFO_TYPE macro originally added by change [advc.enum]. I.e. the vectors are now enumerated at compile time instead of being tracked at runtime.

commit 6d3e7b0f01343f7aa303c8540a88f297ac81ceb9
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-04-06 17:10:54 +0200

Tweak impact of city population on revolt chance

Apply the era-based cap on the population factor _after_ shifting it toward the highest-ever population. And shift the population factor back toward the actual population eventually so that cities that stay small in the long run don't indefinitely get a revolt chance based on the highest-ever population.

Prompted by this post by CFC user Lanstro:
https://forums.civfanatics.com/threads/advanced-civ.614217/page-29#post-15723888

commit b6934e888877c3925a1ccf84de6c27a9d49d9623
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-04-05 23:33:23 +0200

City trade tweaks

[change id advc.ctr]

Show a note in the city-conquered popup when the current target player for liberation differs from the target at peacetime (i.e. when liberation to the original owner is only possible through the city-conquered popup.

AI again (as in BtS) says that "no such deal is possible" when asked what it will give in exchange for liberating a city.

AI more reluctant to liberate a city for free when the recipient is the worst enemy of other relevant AI civs.

Increased trade memory for liberation:
* More lenient thresholds to determine that a liberated city is a major city or insignificant.
* A single city of medium importance results in "+2: You liberated our cities" despite rounding.
* Both sides' trade values are counted for fair trade and enemy trade memory, but still reduced a bit (because there's also liberation memory).
* Fair/ enemy trade memory is also counted when ceding a city through the city-conquered popup.

commit f54cc305502cb61c3a27a6de7f015925a5f1b4c1
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-04-01 18:26:38 +0200

AI code for initiating city trades

Function AI_proposeCityTrade replacing BtS code that had merely allowed the AI to liberate cities.

AI code for war trades moved into a new function AI_proposeWarTrade and modified so that the AI can cede a city in order to hire a war ally.

AI may liberate cities directly after conquest.
Voluntary vassals may no longer cede cities to rivals (same as capitulated vassals now).
Misc. tweaks in city trade AI code, especially concerning vassals.

Also some refactoring in AI_counterPropose and AI_balanceDeal; mainly renamed variables. That "pOurList", "pTheirList" stuff was impossible to work with. (The control flow is still bad.)

And fixed(?) issues with rounding amounts of gold to a multiple of 5 in deals proposed by the AI. [advc.026]

Adjustments to AI trade gold based on possible tech trades [advc.550f] are now disabled when tech trading is disabled.

commit 71da0d54492257bcc1defd78305792e005542dbb

Fix minor BtS bug with unit bumping after city acquisition

commit 507332fd0f239677826a773c0d543a5e9d23b92d
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-03-29 16:47:05 +0200

Peace treaty in tribute/help deals; no treaty for vassals.

When the AI asks for or demands an annual item (gold per turn, resource), a peace treaty gets included in the annual deal. So that all obligations expire together and automatically. (Automatic cancellation of peace treaties at the start of a game turn was already implemented.) Also more transparent: the peace treaty will now appear on the trade table. [advc.104m]

And peace treaties now have a canTradeItem check that prevents vassals from signing one, i.e., after ending a war, only the master gets a peace treaty. (Vassals had already been unwilling to grant rival gift and tribute requests, so this additional change should only be relevant for peace treaties signed at the end of a war.) CvPlayer::forcePeace ignores the canTradeItem check, but I've replaced most forcePeace calls with CvTeam::signPeaceTreaty. forcePeace is used for giving vassals a peace treaty after a peacekeeping vote. [advc.130v]

Revised [advc.032] (peace treaty signed during a peace treaty resets the old peace treaty) and [advc.130p] (trade value of peace not counted for diplo memory) to be compatible with the new changes.

AI code for city trade evaluation

[change id advc.ctr]

Some UWAI code revised and moved into a function AI_assetVal that is now used for both war evaluation and evaluation of city trades. AI_cityTradeVal rewritten, now also taking into account strategic factors like diplo attitude, victory stage and power ratio.

Special treatment for liberated cities -- normally, I want to count no trade value for those (liberation player feels entitled to get it for free), but in some situations I want to count a reduced trade value and in some situations the full trade value.

AI liberation memory based on the city's trade value.

Non-liberation city trades result in a peace treaty.

Tweaks to AI code for proposing city trades. Can now put more than one city on the table and regardless of at-war status. But AI code for initiating city trades is still to be done. Also to be done: More testing, profiling, documentation in manual (partly done), loading screen hints.

commit c3dd918c22d880f408d219139fb1792caa588990
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-02-29 22:54:00 +0100

ScaledInt: Improve accuracy of operators, fix enum type issues.

Multiplication and division now call one of the static mulDiv functions directly instead of first converting the operand with the smaller scale to the larger scale. This leads to more accurate results and may also be faster.

The comparison operators now convert both operands to the product of the two scales (unless the scales are the same), using 64 bit if necessary. This makes comparisons exact - as they should be.

My approach to the EnumType parameter -- defining function templates only for matching types -- doesn't work after all; at least not so easily. For now, matching types are enforced by static assertions.

And I've renamed "scaled_int" to "scaled" and "scaled_uint" to "uscaled". The default scale factor is now 2048. The class template is still named ScaledInt.

commit ca9bcfb831e2d1fd363bcc2f5af3ae43d264fa83
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-02-22 22:21:57 +0100

More work on ScaledInt (to be continued)

Introduce a single mulDiv member function for all multiplications by a ratio; replacing the toScale function.
The global arithmetic operators with two ScaledInt operands are now templates that can accept operands on different scales and of different integer types.
New header TypeChoice.h to allow the functions above to choose their return types based on the operand types.

safeCast function for casts to IntType added.

Workaround for friend bug in MSVC03: Set the scale factor through an int parameter iSCALE; cast iSCALE into a static IntType const member SCALE.

Assertions to detect overflow when rounding to nearest in ScaledInt::round. Open issue: For unsigned IntType, overflow could also occur when rounding in ScaledInt::mulDiv – and would currently go unnoticed.

Remove increasedTo and decreasedTo const functions.
Remove fromRational<int>(int) -- use ScaledInt(int,int) instead.

commit 11d0b2e88af90990389dc4b15f1cea5951fc652c
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-02-19 23:08:17 +0100

Revise ScaledInt class (to be continued)

Prompted by comments by Nightinggale and devolution in this CFC thread:
https://forums.civfanatics.com/threads/class-for-fixed-point-arithmetic.655037/
(But not all comments have been addressed yet.)

Turn static ScaledInt::szBuf from a char array into a CvString and move it to a base class because one instance shared by all ScaledInt types is enough.

Rename static variables MIN and MAX to INTMIN, INTMAX to make clearer that they're the limits of IntType, not of ScaledInt. Now private. Add public functions MIN(), MAX() that return the limits of ScaledInt based on INTMIN and INTMAX.

Changes to template parameters:
* Replace 'class' with 'typename' in template parameter lists.
* Rename the 'INT' parameter to 'IntType', in part, because INT is already defined (as an alias of int) in WinDef.h.
* Switch the order of SCALE and IntType so that SCALE can be of type IntType. Known issue with this: Can't declare template classes with dependent arguments as friend; this appears to be a bug in the MSVC compiler:
https://stackoverflow.com/questions/18666240/friending-a-dependent-argument-template
I've made all private data members public until I can find a workaround.
* Add a template parameter 'EnumType' to allow instantiations that are (on purpose; for type safety) incompatible with each other.
* Remove unnecessary template argument lists.
* Use preprocessor defines to make parameter/ argument lists easier to handle in global and out-of-class definitions.

Define a type LongType depending on the size of IntType for intermediate results that are outside the limits of IntType. (Previously, __int64 was always used, which is wasteful when IntType has fewer than 4 bytes.)

Add some more assertions to check for overflow and signed/unsigned mismatches.

Add uint comparison operators. (Tbd.: Try using function templates instead.)

Add serialization functions that read and write to FDataStreamBase.

Fix an overflow bug in operator/= by forwarding to the toScale function.
In the works: A mulDiv function template to handle operator*=, operator/= and scale conversions. Currently, there is some duplicate code, and operations on mixed scales aren't as accurate as they could be.

ScaledInt uses MulDiv

commit 8f753f67c2a0faa5974fd3890c11979e7d7a06cb
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-02-12 16:44:24 +0100

Treat NoWarProb of Random Pers. as secret; map trade speed tweak.

The AI now treats the NoWarProb values of other AI leaders as secret when playing with Random Personalities. [advc.104y]

When trying to even out an AI-AI tech trade, maps are only considered when they have a significant trade value. [advc.136b]
This was intended as a performance tweak, but it turns out that by far the slowest part of a map trade is the graphics update – and that occurs only when the active human player is involved. Specifically, the update of route graphics is very slow; it seems that the game core DLL can't do anything about that.

And some misc. refactoring.

commit 8672396f414d621e4a72d35fadd0d0ec9b014066
Author: f1rpo <hidden>

commit c16d688b14839d095beefc52209b3a74decf2364
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-02-04 22:56:03 +0100

Class scaled_int for fixed-point math incl. pow function

[change id advc.fract]

Some initial tests for correctness and speed look promising. Seems like scaled_int won't be significantly slower or faster than double (nor did I expect it to be faster). Not sure that I've overloaded all the right operators. Perhaps I'll switch to using boost/operators.hpp later.

The power function (for fractions) could surely be improved in terms of accuracy and speed, but, for my needs, the current implementation may well be sufficient.

I don't think I'll convert all my floating-point code to scaled_int, or at least not the UWAI component. The interface of scaled_int is intended to be rich enough though to make that conversion feasible if necessary.

I intend to use scaled_int in modified BtS code in order to make my changes portable to mods that don't use floating-point numbers. ScaledInt.h itself should be pretty easily portable.
(But I won't change the rounding behavior in places that players can and do observe, e.g. +25% from Forge will continue to amount to 0 when the city's base production rate is less than 4.)

Ties in with this "We the People" issue (and #169):
https://github.com/We-the-People-civ4col-mod/Mod/issues/176

commit 206f9c6a96fdafc1985b34e304438327304082be
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-01-24 16:01:55 +0100

Merge FLTA class with its base; resolve type cast problems.

[change id 003u]

Removed the unused FFreeListArray class. That left only FFreeListTrashArray (short: FLTA) and its base class FFreeListArrayBase. I've merged them into a single class to facilitate further changes.

Moved all FLTA code that calls member functions of the template argument from the FLTA header file into an implementation file and added explicit instantiations. This way, client code can call the iteration functions (beginIter, nextIter, getAt) without having to include the headers that define the template argument. In that same vein, FLTA now has an optional second template parameter for a base class type and can return pointers to that base class type. For example, FLTA<CvUnit,CvUnitAI>::getAt returns a CvUnit* and AI_getAt a CvUnitAI*.

commit c9091ad668097a0f4d2d80f8e7ee837768a93de9
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-01-22 23:05:18 +0100

Style changes in FFreeListTrashArray.h

commit f0551b743bd82d3c92fbf1ea405a566ef3d2e6b8
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-01-22 22:38:16 +0100

Inlined getUnit(IDInfo), getCity(IDInfo)

IDInfo structs that represent no city or no unit now store the id of the Barbarian player instead of NO_PLAYER. The FreeList id is set to FFreeList::INVALID_INDEX as before. This change makes the NO_PLAYER check in getUnit and getCity (and AI_getUnit, AI_getCity) unnecessary and makes it worth inlining those functions. I've moved them from CvGameCoreUtils to CvPlayer(AI).h (still as global functions) for that purpose - CvGameCoreUtils.h doesn't (and shouldn't) include CvPlayer.h. For files that need to call getCity or getUnit, don't include CvPlayer.h and don't care much about performance, I've added static wrapper functions named "fromIDInfo" to CvCity, CvCityAI, CvUnit and CvUnitAI.

Also removed an unnecessary NULL check from FreeListTrashArray::getAt.

commit 78c81ecb9afd3af6d0cccd77b92c88ca0c689087

commit 09ea6a2887242534b481c854f4807faac12af2fc
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-01-17 02:37:34 +0100

Adjust/ fix decay probabilities of AI diplo memory counters

Changed the formula for faster decay when multiple diplo actions are remembered. For example, two declarations of war should now be fully forgotten after an expected duration of 150+75 turns on Normal speed. Three declarations after 150+75+50 turns. Etc. My old formula was ... more complicated and incorrectly implemented. [advc.130r]

Memory about tech trades ("you're becoming too advanced", "you shared your discoveries") are exempt from the above, i.e. they decay as in BtS with a per-turn probability that doesn't depend on the memory count. However, for "you shared your discoveries", I've increased the per-turn probability by a factor of 4, meaning that one tech is forgotten, on average, every 25 turns instead of every 100 turns. The relations modifier per tech is now between 0.08 and 0.2 (was between 0.05 and 0.2) and fractions get rounded to the nearest integer. (The rounding change is not part of this commit; has been in place since v0.8 or so. BtS rounds down.) On the bottom line, +1 from shared discoveries should be easier to get than in BtS, but more difficult to sustain. (Not sure about higher modifiers.) Still to be tested. [advc.553]

"You gave us help" now decays with a (base) probability of 1/150; was 1/200. [advc.144]

commit de7f9344698fe504740b11a6864b454821347a51
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-01-16 19:48:20 +0100

Performance tweaks: plotDanger, isEnemy

Merged CvPlayerAI::AI_getPlotDanger and AI_getAnyPlotDanger into a single function and made various small performance tweaks.

Overloaded CvUnit::isEnemy(TeamTypes,CvPlot const*) so that the original function doesn't have to check whether for NO_TEAM and NULL. isEnemy is checked extremely frequently, but, since always-hostile units (Privateers) have to be supported, there isn't much one can do to make it faster.

At a few call locations of isEnemy, I've changed call parameters because the original parameters didn't quite make sense to me. Those changes should only be relevant when a mod-mod gives the always-hostile ability to additional units.

Renamed or replaced a bunch of functions:
CvPlayerAI::AI_getAnyPlotDanger -> AI_isAnyPlotDanger
CvUnit::isPotentialEnemy -> CvUnitAI::AI_isPotentialEnemyOf
CvUnit::potentialWarAction -> CvUnitAI::AI_mayAttack(CvPlot const&)
CvUnitAI::AI_potentialEnemy -> CvUnitAI::AI_mayAttack(TeamTypes,CvPlot const&)
isPotentialEnemy (CvGameCoreUtils) -> CvTeamAI::AI_mayAttack(TeamTypes)
CvPlot::isVisiblePotentialEnemyUnit -> CvTeamAI::AI_mayAttack(CvPlot const&)
CvPlot::getNumVisiblePotentialEnemyDefenders -> CvUnitAI::AI_countEnemyDefenders/ AI_isAnyEnemyDefender
CvSelectionGroup::isHasPathToAreaEnemyCity -> CvSelectionGroupAI::AI_isHasPathToAreaEnemyCity

CvUnit now uses EnumMaps. CvUnit.h includes CvInfo_Unit.h so that the forwarding functions can be inlined.

commit e4ff624d23ec90298119911fa182fa0e4c870d5a
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-01-13 19:37:36 +0100

Remove or replace unnecessary AI_plotValid calls

Replaced some calls to CvUnitAI::AI_plotValid with simpler (inline) checks and removed calls that were (mostly) redundant.
As a side-effect, the (unused) NoRevealMap ability probably performs worse now, and CanMoveAllTerrain (also unused) has even worse AI support than before. CanMoveImpassable (submarines) should still be fully supported.

And some other optimizations for can-unit-enter-tile checks; in particular:
CvUnit caches the unit's domain type and bMoveImpassable; getters inlined. [This will be reverted in the next commit. It's better to just include CvUnitInfo.h in CvUnit.h and then inline all the wrapper-getters.]
CvCity and CvUnit cache a pointer to their current plot.

Added functions CvUnit::getPlot and CvCity::getPlot that return a CvPlot reference. Replaced all occurrences of "plot()->" with "getPlot()." and, for good measure, also "area()->" with "getArea()." Changed a few function signatures from CvPlot pointer to reference, but most of them still take pointers.

Renamed a few functions:
CvUnitInfo::isCanMoveAllTerrain -> canMoveAllTerrain
CvUnit::canEnterArea -> canEnterTerritory
CvUnit::canLoad -> canLoadOntoAnyUnit

commit e16073216cf47de65c28eccba85dc0d3148c1c31
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-01-13 06:33:01 +0100

Fix recently introduced bug (operator precedence)

commit 7e5b97aa288afedf7d8c8a63ac397fb6b4415d91
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-01-12 01:21:56 +0100

More CvInfo signature changes, Python wrappers.

Changed some more CvInfo function signatures from int to enum types. (Still many more to go  ... someday.)
Changing int parameters to enum types breaks Python code, e.g.
	for k in range(gc.getNumFeatureInfos()):
		if (gc.getBuildInfo(j).getFeatureTech(k) == i): # ...
in CvTechChooser.py. Instead of getFeatureTech(FeatureTypes), an adapter function getFeatureTech(int) needs to be exposed. Unlike most other Python-exposed classes, the CvInfo classes don't have "Cy..." wrapper classes - and I don't want to add those classes either. I've instead declared my adapters as private member functions of the existing CvInfo classes (so far only in CvBuildInfo, CvBuildingInfo) - through a helper macro in CyInfoWrapper.h (new header file).

commit 0a0d96d35e96a36362f97cc0c8bb291549ab4ccc
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-01-12 01:11:00 +0100

Fix AdvCiv bug in AI evaluation of AP

Turns out that the K-Mod code I fixed in
SHA c412a412f5fd0e235147d2bb8e00df865a1ff309
wasn't used at all due to a bug that I introduced several releases ago. As a result the AI hadn't taken the Apostolic Palace's production bonus into account when evaluating Temples and Monasteries.

commit c412a412f5fd0e235147d2bb8e00df865a1ff309
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-01-11 01:03:52 +0100

Fix inconsequential bug in K-Mod AI evaluation of AP production

commit d78d567f9b7fd344d1a0e12b8632e69ccd2d1a25
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-01-10 19:12:48 +0100

Refactoring: randomized iteration, move wonder class checks.

Use CityPlotRandIter for random-order iterations over the tiles in a city radius. [advc.enum]
(Mostly in CvGame.cpp - starting site normalization.)

FOR_EACH macro (in CvInfoEnums.h) that goes through an info enum in a randomized order; e.g.
FOR_EACH_ENUM_RAND(Bonus, GC.getGame().getSorenRand()) {  ... }
[advc.enum]
Not much used yet, but could be useful for avoiding biases toward low enum values in AI code.
The randomization code (CvGameCoreUtils) includes Erik's implementation of std::iota from this commit:
ErikSandbraaten/AdvCiv@c85fd69372b5c1e5eca84614471de17915f4b1a2

Also added a FOR_EACH_ENUM_REV macro that goes through the enum values in descending order.

Removed the global functions (CvGameCoreUtils) that check uniqueness limitations of buildings, projects and units; e.g. isWorldWonderClass(BuildingClassTypes). Instead, I've added member functions to CvBuildingClassInfo, CvUnitClassInfo, CvProjectInfo and wrappers at CvBuildingInfo and CvUnitInfo; e.g. CvBuildingInfo::isWorldWonder(void). [advc.003w]
This allows those functions to be inlined without messing up header dependencies.

Also moved the global "needsArticle" functions introduced by [advc.008e] to CvBuildingInfo and CvProjectInfo. (Those functions say whether the name of a wonder or project grammatically requires an article.)

Changed the return types of some frequently (and not so frequently) used CvInfo functions from int to an enum type. [advc.003x]
Related "We the People" issue:
https://github.com/We-the-People-civ4col-mod/Mod/issues/302

Fixed a BtS bug with the unused AdvancedStartCostIncrease XML tags for improvements and routes. They had increased the Advanced Start cost based on improvements or routes owned by any player – but only those placed by the current player should count.

And some misc. refactoring in CvGameTextMgr.

commit 279c9d078008359b9fa4680e1146c35c68f345cc
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-01-04 17:23:25 +0100

Tweaks to AI code for automated air recon

[change id advc.029]

Consider the full recon range around each candidate target; BBAI had only looked at the adjacent tiles.

Non-visible water tiles aren't preferred. (It seems that the BBAI code was mainly intended for spotting incoming transports.) Prefer exploration near non-friendly tiles and especially near enemy tiles instead. (I expect that exploration on water will still be the most common result; there tend to be a lot of non-visible water tiles.)

Don't prefer to target tiles at maximal range.

commit 70b59ebcbd6f9dd0d1e1069cfcae537b9a8e14d2
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-01-04 15:05:23 +0100

Fix BBAI bugs in automated air recon

In the code that selects the recon target tile, a visibility check was flipped. As a result, the target was chosen randomly with a bias only for maximal distance from the airbase.

Also, tiles around the airbase had been evaluated instead of tiles around the candidate recon tile.

BBAI uses its AI air recon function only for automated human units, but change [advc.650] also lets the AI use it.

Even with the bugs fixed, it's not a good heuristic. I'll make some tweaks in a separate commit.

Fix recent bug in AI team attitude; tweak OB attitude check.

My code had sometimes applied the conversion from relations value (total of all modifiers) to attitude value twice, which usually resulted in Cautious attitude.

Improved the performance of the "we would have nothing to gain" check (when there is no revealed land tile). [advc.124].

commit b96465d167c7fa35f0a8d974eccd457d9dcb7edb
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2020-01-03 02:18:48 +0100

Refactor CvArea getter functions

Changed CvArea function parameters from CvArea* and (occasionally) int to CvArea const& when possible. Repurposed the getArea functions (CvPlot, CvCity, CvUnit) to return a CvArea&. Same-area checks are now usually performed by functions 'sameArea', e.g. CvCity::sameArea(CvCity const&), or isArea(CvArea const&). The 'area' functions (CvPlot, CvCity, CvUnit, CvSelectionGroup) aren't used much anymore, but are retained for compatibility with other DLL mods. (The change to the getArea functions will hurt compatibility though.) All functions mentioned above are inlined except CvSelectionGroup::area. Previously, most of them weren't inlined because CvPlot and CvArea objects had to be looked up in CvMap. Now CvPlot, CvCity and CvUnit each store a CvArea*, and CvPlot no longer stores an area id.
(The 'plot' functions still aren't inlined; same problem. Also the 'getTeam' functions.)

And I've inlined some CvUnit getter functions.

Revised AI calculation of culture garrisons

More accurate estimate of the number of units needed in a city in order to bring the revolt chance down to an acceptable risk. The per-area target for the number of floating defenders is increased a bit based on culture pressure. In cases that look hopeless, no extra defenders are allotted. [advc.099c]

(Not sure how quickly the AI will be able to reinforce cities that are about to revolt. AI units in non-defensive roles still don't respond to imminent revolts. The AI also still makes no use of the revolt protection from the Leadership promotion.)

commit 9f296435d19adf72f15a4bc2f0ccec1c70982b88
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2019-12-30 00:52:01 +0100

Update player AI cache data after loading savegame

The cache in question contains some misc. CvPlayerAI data and was introduced by K-Mod. Updating the cache after loading means that the cache data don't necessarily have to be serialized. The cache normally gets updated at the start of a player's turn. That means, only the cache of the active human player didn't get updated after loading. The human cache matters for testing through AI Auto Play, so, for development purposes this is an important bugfix. (All the K-Mod cache data was being serialized, so the error had been mine alone.)

commit 5c7f7404c5292bad04b44907dbeae6dea4bcc3d6
Author: f1rpo <hidden>

Only human players can reuse a dead player as a colonial vassal

[change id dlph.24]

Kek-Mod disallows reusing dead players entirely because "this can cause weird bugs sometimes".
DarkLunaPhantom/Civ4-K-Mod@69b8a18ed7daa7271de44593a15ab5b423120bc0
There's also a conflict with AdvCiv's Immortal Culture component. I'd like to fix those problems, but, for the time being, it's safer to disable recycled player slots. However, I don't want to disable colonial vassals entirely in 18-player games. If a human player creates a vassal and it doesn't work correctly, that player can (hopefully) reload an earlier savegame. An AI player creating a vassal could be much harder to repair.

commit 6a5b64619a66fbda417f4793f6edaa25787bf51e

commit 8a3cf7d4606d71b4dc5cca74a7103ff24f7c7355
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2019-12-28 22:49:03 +0100

AI may pay for peace even if it thinks that both sides want it

(UWAI component)

Previously, an AI civ had never paid a human player for peace if it thought that peace was in both sides' interest. Now the AI will pay based on the difference between AI peace utility and two times the human peace utility. (It won't pay if that difference isn't positive.)

Thanks to everyone on the CFC forums who had complained about the old behavior. It was too reliant on what the AI "thought" about the human player's situation, and it's not a reasonable position to insist on a square peace treaty when continuing the war is disastrous for one side and merely inconvenient for the other.

The AI is now also a bit more generous when paying for peace in situations when the war is going badly but not extremely badly. (For extremely bad wars, the AI still assumes that peace is unlikely to last and therefore not worth very much.)

To address problems with AI civs insisting on reparations from much more powerful (human) war enemies.

commit fb1794179682ba78647f63ea54fe6b2870b6a08c
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2019-12-26 22:30:02 +0100

Change culture garrison strength formula; add tooltip.

Culture garrison strength is now equal to current combat strength with the following modifiers:
- Defensive modifiers (Archery units, Garrison promotions)
- Generic strength modifiers (Combat promotions)
- Minus 50% for non-lethal units (i.e. Siege except Machine Gun).
iCultureGarrison in XML is obsolete.

Adjusted the foreign culture strength formula to balance out the change to culture garrison strength, and to make revolts a bit easier to suppress in the midgame and a bit harder in the late game. Added two XML parameters for fine-tuning this.

Added hover text on the main map and city screen (nationality bar) showing how much more garrison strength is needed to reduce a city's revolt chance to 0, or how much garrison strength can be safely removed.

commit 4d0dc0f4fa1e90103f76e6134d4d267659d10c67
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2019-12-25 21:56:39 +0100

Allow air unit production in cities with insufficient capacity

[change id advc.001b]

Basically back to how it worked in BtS. The AdvCiv rule can still be enabled through GlobalDefines_advc.xml.

Unlike in BtS, units that are force-moved upon completion have their movement points spent. When a (legal) rally point is set, force-moved units are sent there. When no rally point is set, units are moved to the nearest legal city or fort (or destroyed if there is none) as in BtS and a message is shown on the main interface.

commit 891273d300ade2b2adc69bb9cc0a907c74e59267
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2019-12-21 13:19:32 +0100

AI city placement tweaks

[change id advc.031]

Some special discouragement from settling on top of a (high-yield) resource is needed after all. And fixed various other issues introduced recently when moving/ refactoring the city site evaluation code, including crashes on flat maps.

The tech checks and yield computation for resources were still incorrect in several ways.

Made the placement of the second AI city less strategic (i.e. less about preempting a neighbor) and thus more focused on tile yields etc.

Resources on tiles that the AI hopes to flip are no longer ignored.

Adjustments to resource trade values, especially in the early game.

No Lighthouse food assumed when settling in a non-coastal tile.

The food modifier computed from resource yields no longer applies to non-resource tiles.

Reduced the impact of the general low-food modifier.

Stricter bad-health check to discourage early Jungle cities.

Chopping yield is no longer assumed for features that probably shouldn't be chopped (Tundra Forests).

Account for features that don't allow improvements (Oasis).

Distance penalties reduced.

Fixed a small issue with the detection of deadlocked resources that had sometimes discouraged the AI from colonizing an island chain.

City placement in Advanced Start assumes that borders are easy to expand.

Various adjustments to culture modifiers for contested tiles.

Various small tweaks.

AI exploration units now prioritize the surroundings of (tentative) city sites. [advc.031d]

commit df90424a0ca392598403e322a22010311fa5d01c

[change id advc.031]

The food modifier is now applied along with the other modifiers at the end of the evaluation.

I wanted to remove the "special yields" array, but backed down when it got too complicated. Instead added some comments.

commit 078fc9bd7c7962bdd944073c372ffd2e5853b16e
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2019-12-15 23:33:35 +0100

Move AI_foundValue computation into a new class

Also split the code into subroutines. This has mandated -or strongly suggested- other refactoring changes to the found value code.

I've taken this opportunity to add logging code. When the log is enabled through BBAILog.h, a breakdown of the found value computation is written to the BBAI log file whenever an AI player founds a city and when placing or normalizing a starting location. When an AI city is founded, breakdowns are also written for the found value of the best adjacent tile and for the next best city site overall. [advc.031c]

The few logs I've looked at and the refactoring changes have brought to light or reminded me of some minor issues, which I've fixed: [advc.031]
* Improved evaluation of chopping (was previously factored into the health evaluation).
* Revised tech requirement checks for resources (techs for revealing, improving, trading the resource).
* Removed a K-Mod kludge (negative yields assumed on the city tile) that was supposed to discourage the AI from settling on top of resources and flood plains. Hopefully no longer needed.
* Disabled K-Mod code that was supposed to steer the distribution of starting locations among landmasses. As far as I can tell, CvPlayer::findStartingArea handles that.
* Made the AI more pessimistic about flipping tiles near a colony on a continent where a rival already has multiple cities.
(Thanks to keldath for making me aware of that potential problem a while ago.)
* Don't count on getting the upper hand culturally when both sides have the Creative trait.
* Disabled some minor (probably inadvertent) visibility cheats.
* Made the AI reluctant to move its starting Settler more than 1 tile on maps with a higher-than-normal player count (e.g. more than 8 on a Standard-size map with Medium sea level). To make situations like the one reported here by Elkad
https://forums.civfanatics.com/threads/advanced-civ.614217/page-22#post-15602780
more unlikely. [advc.108]
* Slightly increased production yield weight for Barbarian cities (no bias previously). [advc.303]
* Some tweaks in the evaluation of sea access.
* Minor bugfix: AI personality had sometimes affected city site evaluation when computing recommendations for humans and when placing starting locations.

Only tangentially related:
Choosing the "Balanced resources" map option now causes more aggressive normalization of starting locations. In particular, unrevealed resources are then taken into account. [advc.108]

commit b2b7802dd311c71630e9c2fad45e750ed2d93114
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2019-12-12 01:55:15 +0100

Field-of-view slider tweaks

[change id advc.090]

Slightly longer bar (at least on high resolutions), which makes it a bit easier to set the slider to a particular value. However, the issue described here (at the end of the post)
https://forums.civfanatics.com/threads/advanced-civ.614217/page-24#post-15610934
isn't (and probably won't be) fixed.

The text labels show a percentage (10 to 100%) instead of the true FoV value. From 10 to 75%, the percentage is equal to the true FoV value, above 75%, the slider becomes more sensitive, which should allow Huge maps to be fully displayed (e.g. for AI Auto Play or a screenshot).

commit d69859907a71e062be91500b529701e0d22d5c39
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2019-12-11 23:53:43 +0100

Options for replay minimap texture

When the HOF_STORE_REPLAYS_AS_BTS option is not enabled, then replay files store the MINIMAP_RENDER_SIZE value that was used to generate the terrain texture. This allows players to change the texture resolution without breaking compatibility. [advc.106m]

REPLAY_TEXTURE_ERA option in XML that allows storing a terrain texture for the replay file before the game is over so that the replay minimap doesn't reflect e.g. terrain changes from global warming. [advc.106n]

See GlobalDefines_advc.xml for more info about these two changes (updated manual yet to be committed). There are also related posts in the AdvCiv thread on CFC:
https://forums.civfanatics.com/threads/advanced-civ.614217/page-24#post-15610743

The column with the "start replay" buttons is hidden when the Hall of Fame screen is entered at the end of a game. (Replays can't be viewed at that point; the buttons had no effect.) [advc.106i]

Increased initial camera distance a bit. [advc.004m]
(Seems to be affected by the screen resolution; I guess most players use Full HD by now.)

And some rather minor refactoring in CvReplayInfo.cpp.

commit 488e1af47d4685c73f2f3d27b8826cb6a8fae6a5

Amend some (not-so-recent) changes to defender selection

Siege units can no longer kill workers and non-land units in cities when all defenders are maximally damaged. This was an unintended side-effect of restructuring code for selecting the best city defender. [advc.089]

Amendment to change [advc.028] (invisible submarines can come forward to protect a weaker defender):
Submarines only step in when they can kill the attacker, i.e. not against air attacks.

Amendment to change [advc.315b] (Explorer can attack Barbarians but can't capture cities):
Help text no longer says that Explorer can't capture units. (It's supposed to be able to steal Barbarian workers - if worker stealing is re-enabled; currently disabled by change advc.010.)
