commit bb97a41a85637a352da2c562796b4d67f839a80d
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2018-12-26 21:35:47 +0100

UI support for partial worker builds added

If Ctrl is held down while issuing a command to a Worker, the build is stopped one turn before completion. ("pre-build")

If Ctrl is held down while hovering over a tile, the progress on all unfinished builds is shown.

These additions address the following open K-Mod issue:
https://github.com/karadoc/Civ4-K-Mod/issues/1
(Now that I've amended this commit twice, I realize that I shouldn't have included the Issue link. GitHub has pinned each of my amendments to the Issue. Sorry.)

I've taken up boeti's suggestion (or maybe I had the same thought independently) with the modifier key.
My implementation is safe for multiplayer - in principle, I've only done one quick test. The whole thing isn't properly tested yet.

Progress on unfinished builds still decays through change [advc.011] if the tile is left alone for too long. "Too long" is now 8 turns; was 5 turns before.

I've adopted some code from BULL. (Can't find out the specific author.) These adoptions are marked with [advc.011c], whereas my original code is marked with [advc.011b].

commit 470df8df939b720d8fab879baccfb23c0b093ce2
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2018-12-22 22:59:07 +0100

Changes to handicap settings to improve balance and pace, and some misc. changes

Changes to Civ4HandicapInfos.xml (see manual for details): [advc.251]

* On Emperor difficulty and above, human civs pay higher production costs and face higher city growth and GP thresholds. AI production discounts have been lowered. Added a new AI discount that lowers GP thresholds on Monarch difficulty and above. These changes should make the various yields more balanced and reduce the number of units on the map.
* Human production costs, research costs and GP thresholds are rounded to the nearest multiple of 5.
* Replaced the multiplicative per-era modifier for AI discounts with an additive modifier based on the game turn number. The new modifier applies to AI production costs, research costs and city growth thresholds. (Whereas the per-era modifier had applied to production costs, city growth thresholds, expenses and war weariness. An earlier change [advc.250d] had already uncoupled AI upgrade costs and unit supply from the per-era modifier.)
* (Human) civic upkeep increased on all difficulty levels below Monarch; now the same progression as inflation. Increased the AI civic upkeep and unit cost modifiers on Deity from 60% to 75% (meaning that the Deity AI has to pay higher civic upkeep and unit cost than in BtS).

The BBAI era modifier for tech costs (Civ4EraInfos.xml) is now actually applied to tech costs; was previously applied to research rates. [advc.910]
Likewise, the AdvCiv adjustments for the "No Tech Trading" [advc.550d] and "Raging Barbarians" [advc.308] game options now apply to tech costs instead of research rates.
No research bonus for knowing a single prereq tech. I've instead reduced all tech costs by about 20% through the era modifier. [advc.910]
Changed the game year computation on Normal speed so that the years pass a bit faster in Renaissance. This should match the historical time line better. [advc.910]
Tech cost of Iron Working increased a little. [advc.031b]

Changes to free units on the highest two difficulty levels: [advc.250e]
* Reduced the number of free Archers that the AI receives at game start by 1 on Immortal and Deity difficulty.
* Reduced the number of defensive units placed in Barbarian cities from 4 to 3 on Immortal difficulty.

AI floating defenders are no longer double adjusted to the AI cost discounts. I had added such an adjustment as part of [advc.107] without realizing that it was redundant.

Three changes inspired by this post and the next by Akbarthegreat in the K-Mod subforum:
https://forums.civfanatics.com/threads/k-mod-far-beyond-the-sword.407049/page-149#post-15311568
* When the AI cancels a vassal agreement, the diplo text states the reason (e.g. "we're doing fine on our own now"). [advc.062]
* When a civ is close to a Domination victory, vassals of that civ which are Pleased with their master don't break away, even if their power rating is above the global average. [advc.112]
* Bad terrain is never replaced under a precious resource (Gold/Silver/Gems) and a bad feature on a precious resource is only removed in the inner ring around the starting location. [advc.108]

Slightly decreased the size of the Modern Armor 3D model. [advc.002j]

Reworded the help text for the InstanceCostModifier ability, though the ability is still unused. (I could see myself using it for War Elephant.) [advc.004g]

Enabled an assertion in CvXMLLoadUtility that reports when a GetChildXmlValByName call fails. This reveals typos in the CvInfos::read(CvXMLLoadUtility*) functions, which can otherwise easily go undetected. [advc.006b]

Const qualifier added to several CvMap functions. [advc.003]

Added a check in the InvasionGraph class (UWAI) to prevent a crash when playing from an old (pre-v0.93?) savegame.

Some refactoring in CvGameTextMgr::setTechHelp. [advc.003]

commit 662e020a56765e5df4cee3ac8769079829d61cb4
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2018-12-22 13:07:25 +0100

Tentative changes to make naval invasions easier to intercept

On the turn that war is declared, units that enter enemy borders have all their movement points spent. (Except air units and units that can explore rival territory.) [advc.162]

Units that get "bumped" out of foreign territory have all their movement points spent. They're also woken up and de-automated (if they had e.g. been set to auto-explore). {advc.163]

Drill I enables the Blitz promotion, but Blitz allows only one extra attack per turn (was previously limited only by the unit's movement points).  [advc.164]

Fixed some issues with routes in the fog of war that I came across while implementing the above:
The pathfinder waypoints no longer give away unrevealed routes on revealed tiles, and the tile danger computations of the AI respect fog of war for routes. [advc.001i]

commit cbb4852bb0a72aefb11307b1c0ef18dfffcc310c
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2018-12-16 23:07:31 +0100

Cppcheck (refactoring, minor bugfixes), bugs fixed in naval AI

Ran the whole DLL code base through Cppcheck (code analysis) and went through the ca. 1000 (style) warnings. A lot of these were clearly not worth addressing (e.g. constructors with single arguments) or false positives. The ones I did address were mostly shadowed variables, redundant code and "scope can be reduced," resulting from the C-style variable declarations at the start of a function. I've moved most of the declarations that Cppcheck objected to because they had been bothering me for some time. Most of these stylistic changes I haven't marked in the code because that would defeat the purpose of making the code easier read. [advc.003i]

Unused functions and classes: I've left the majority of these alone (apart from adding a comment), but some I've commented out, removed through the preprocessor or deleted. [advc.003j]

While I've come across a few dozen apparent oversights, very few of these had actually resulted in erroneous behavior, and the few that did, look like very minor bugs: [advc.001]
- CvPlayerAI::AI_doDiplo: A mixup of two variables had made the AI reluctant to give away more than one technology in an (inter-AI) war trade.
- CvUnitAI::AI_assaultSeaTransport: In the AI code for choosing the tile to unload on, a condition that discourages unloading on the wrong side of a river had had no effect.
- Fractal.cpp::tectonicAction: This looks like two variables were mixed up, but I'm not sure that my version is correct either. The code is used (only?) by the Continents map script.

Unrelated to the above, I've encountered and fixed several issues in the naval AI resulting from [advc.017b] (and BBAI code) that had sometimes caused the AI to train way too many Galleys on certain maps (especially Pangaea).

While I was at it: The AI now trains fewer sea explorers (typically just 1 instead of 2) if the map script's file name is "Pangaea". [advc.017]

The new code uses the available space more efficiently, sorts the units more sensibly and shows the total number of military land units and ships. [advc.061]

commit b62c4000571695a9d6ccc5c462fd606f4f172d33
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2018-12-12 13:13:59 +0100

XML cache disabled
[advc.003i]

It appears that the cache is never written when a mod is loaded, meaning that the cache accomplishes nothing and the write/read attempts only waste time (though probably very little). On top of that, it's possible that, in rare cases, cache data written by the unmodded game is read by the mod, which will result in errors.

Credit to Nightinggale for telling me me how to disable it:
https://forums.civfanatics.com/threads/avoid-creating-bugs-from-the-exe-calling-the-dll.639167/#post-15297196

commit d4fd8b627725487982f600b0f7f9327fa3756119
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2018-11-20 13:41:52 +0100

More stop-trading tweaks, UI tweaks (combat odds, animations, city alerts), a few minor bugfixes (AdvCiv,K-Mod)

Stop-trading resolutions (UN, AP) cancel recently signed deals, and such deals don't prevent the resolution from being proposed. Exception: The civ that proposes the resolution mustn't have deals with the target that are too recent to be canceled.
AI civs vote against the resolution if they have deals that are too recent. [advc.130f]
(This behavior is consistent with the changes to war resolutions [dlph.25] in a previous commit and is consistent with regular embargoes canceling recently signed deals.)

The AI refuses to stop trading ("not right now") when this would cancel reparations paid for a peace treaty. [advc.130f]

Amended AI refusal to stop trading: No longer fear the "military might" of a vassal. [advc.130f]

Bug (sign error) fixed in a recent change to attitude thresholds for embargoes. [advc.130f]

Fixed a (likely) UI bug in K-Mod code that had sometimes caused a neutral unit to be shown on the map instead of an enemy unit when neutral and enemy units occupied the same tile (making the enemy unit easy to miss). [advc.001]

Fixed a problem with oscillation of tile ownership in [advc.099b].
For performance reasons, the total culture in a tile is now stored in savegames and a function getTotalCulture replaces countTotalCulture in the CvPlot class. [advc.003b]

Improved the AI evaluation of deals where the AI receives two health resources or two happiness resources from a human. (The AI still refuses to trade more than two.) [advc.036]

Changes to combat odds help text: [advc.048]
* Odds help text shows which unit (short description incl. current and maximal strength and promotions) is about to defend and, if multiple units are selected, which one is about to attack.
* Hitpoints of the combatants are no longer shown separately.
* Advanced Combat Odds (ACO) already had options for showing the attacking and defending unit. These are now enabled by default. ACO is still disabled by default.
* Replaced a few uses of the word "retreat" with "withdraw" in combat odds and other help text. ("Withdraw" is the proper term.)

Combat animations become shorter with each era. Configurable through GlobalDefinesAlt.xml. [advc.002m]

Fixed an oversight in AdvCiv code that had prevented the declare-war popup from appearing when a player attempted to attack an enemy unit on the territory of a non-friendly third party. [advc.001]

The "pending" alerts only trigger when a negative event is about to happen: when a city is about to become unhappy or unhealthy or is about to shrink. About-to-become-unhappy now enabled by default (this only affects new installations of AdvCiv). [advc.106d]

commit a8a7e75c6cde190a0d71297d80ca8f68d064faa0
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2018-11-17 17:51:06 +0100

Some bugfixes (in K-Mod, Kek-Mod, AdvCiv code)

Fixed issues with [dlph.25]: It seems that war votes against a voting member of the AP had been impossible because the game was checking if all voting members - including the war target - were able to declare war. This is now only checked for full members, only full members declare war when the resolution passes and only full members can defy a war vote. Moreover, only peace treaties of the AP leader can block war votes, i.e. the AP overrules peace treaties of other members. An AI civ that has a peace treaty with the target will try to honor the treaty by voting against the war.

Fixed a K-Mod bug in CvPlayerAI::AI_baseBonusVal. The AI had failed to recognize units as obsolete when evaluating a strategic resource that it didn't have access to yet. This had sometimes led the AI to cancel and renew trades for strategic resources (especially Horse) every 10 turns. [advc.001u]

A trade embargo sets stopped-trading-recent memory to 2 rather than adding 2, meaning that multiple embargoes don't stack. [advc.130f]

Amended [advc.106f] so that a canceled defensive pact is no longer doubly announced to the civ that cancels it.

"No Random Events" enabled by default. [advc.104z]

For debugging the UWAI component: Added a function WarAndPeaceAI::Team::doWarReport.

commit 459038215daf879fade2510cc8d3aae4de4ebee8
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2018-11-14 19:36:25 +0100

AI tweaks: embargoes, Barbarian unit AI, defying AP peace

Changes to trade embargoes: [advc.130f]
* Changed my mind again about embargo duration: Now 18 turns on average (15 in a recent commit, 22 in v0.93, 30 in BtS).
* When a war ends, stopped-trading-recently memory of all war parties is reduced to 1, meaning that embargoed war parties become willing to trade, on average, 9 turns after the war ends.
* The threshold for the AI attitude toward the embargo target can be decreased by one level if the attitude toward the player who proposes the embargo is one level higher than required (i.e., in most cases, when Friendly). The AI still won't stop trading when Pleased with the target.
* AI refuses to stop trading with civs that pose an immediate danger: "We're afraid of their military might". And I've increased the impact of power ratio on the embargo trade value.
* Embargo trade value takes into account the number of civs in the game (an embargo is more consequential when there are few civs to trade with).

Fixed an issue introduced with [advc.106d]: The willing-to-talk alert had not been shown when an embargo ended at peacetime.

Small adjustment to the relations penalty for enemy trade: When a non-war party trades with a war enemy, the AI gets upset even if relations with the non-war party are already (almost) as bad as relations with the war enemy. (Previously, to prevent the worst enemy from oscillating, trades between civs that the AI disliked equally had been ignored. Generally, that's still the case; punishing outside help for a war enemy is more important though.) [advc.130p]

Improved the AI code for defying AP resolutions. Was previously only based on war utility, now takes into account the number of cities with the AP religion. Still doesn't check if the anger from defying would actually cause problems. [advc.104n]

Tentatively fixed a K-Mod issue (perhaps also broken in BtS) where the AI declared war without immediately crossing the border. [advc.001t]

Warlords bug fixed: Barbarians no longer target cities protected by the Great Wall. [advc.001]

Simplified the part of change [advc.300] that deals with AI attacks against Barbarian cities, and refactored the part that deals with the behavior of Barbarian attackers. Fixed an oversight that had caused Barbarian units spawned on small continents to behave unaggressively.

Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2018-11-10 13:32:39 +0100

Misc. changes (human/AI handicaps, tile help text)

AI may gift technologies to any liked rivals that have fallen behind. BtS does this only for human rivals ("We see that you could probably use a little help"). And the gifted tech is now chosen with a bias for cheap tech, and Friendly attitude, difficulty and Diplo victory stage play a role. [advc.130z]

Reduced the AI combat bonus against animals from 65% to 50% and also the human combat bonus against animals on difficulty levels below Emperor, e.g. from 50% to 35% on Noble. (These percentages already include the respective bonuses against Barbarians.) [advc.313]

UWAI: The success score that the AI remembers at the end of a war now takes into account paid reparations.

Refactored CvGameTextMgr::setPlotHelp because this 1000-line function was (especially) difficult to navigate. commit 2348464189798cf85b8a1e7763f46fbd552e7227
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2018-11-07 22:11:41 +0100

Balance changes to some ships and a few misc. changes

Ship of the Line requires Iron again (K-Mod had made Copper an alternative), gets +25% vs. Frigate (was 20% in K-Mod, 50% in BtS), +1 speed with Coal and costs 110 production (was 120). I've added a unit ability "SpeedBonuses" to the XML schema for this purpose. [advc.905b]

Ironclad at Steam Power (no Steel required) and +25% defense on Coast (i.e. almost always). [advc.905b]

Attack Submarine and Submarine (now called "Nuclear Submarine") switch places in the tech tree (details: see manual). Both units get 6 first strikes and Destroyer gets to ignore first strikes. Withdrawal chance is again 50% (was 60% in earlier AdvCiv versions). Stealth Destroyer receives the same anti-air and anti-submarine abilities as Destroyer and 1 cargo space for missiles, but also a cost increase from 220 to 270 and has its strength decreased to 30 (same as in BtS; was 33 in earlier AdvCiv versions and 36 in K-Mod). [advc.906]

Increased the base cost of the force-religion and force-civics espionage missions by 25%. [advc.132]

Amended my code for AI strike avoidance after realizing that the strike counter isn't reset in between strikes. [advc.131]

Bug fixed in AI deal proposals: After balancing a deal out with gold per turn, the AI had not always realized that the deal was square and had asked for an additional item (e.g. a technology). I think this is also broken in K-Mod, but probably matters very rarely because K-Mod makes less gold per turn available for trade than AdvCiv. [advc.001]

UWAI:
* Increased the negative war utility counted for ill will from militarily powerful partners of the war target.
* Increased the granularity of the score remembered by the AI at the end of a war. The duration of the war is now taken into account in addition to war successes.

Pillaged improvements are announced immediately (bForce=true). [advc.106j]

Tweaked the 3D model sizes of ships. [advc.002j]

Civilopedia: The box containing strategy advice and historical info has the title "Background" and the heading of the historical section is "History". Previously, it was the other way around. [advc.004y]

commit dc91730714d0c6195d5b9e35daadb7daa3b95ff5
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2018-11-05 13:10:45 +0100

Mostly bugfixes in AdvCiv code
(DLL included, still working toward v0.94)

Fixed an inconsistency in [advc.036] (AI resource trades). Might fix this issue:
https://forums.civfanatics.com/threads/advanced-civ.614217/page-5#post-15127487

Fixed an inconsistency in UWAI that had sometimes made the AI unconcerned about falling behind in score.

Corrected an oversight in [advc.179]: The AI evaluation of the production bonus from Apostolic Palace had been skipped when the AI was at stage 0 of the Diplomacy victory.

Fixed a bug introduced by [advc.132] that had caused the button for the force-religion and force-civics espionage missions to be absent sometimes.

The force-religion espionage mission now requires the target religion to be present in the city where the mission is executed. Increased the impact of the number of cities with the target religion on the mission cost. The AI doesn't use the force-religion mission if the target religion has few adherents -- same threshold as the "too few of our people follow that faith" response, and I've tweaked that threshold a bit. (Previously, force-religion missions by the AI had led to a high number of back-and-forth conversions.) [advc.132]

AI no longer incites revolts in undefended cities, nor when the defenders are greatly outnumbered. [advc.120b]

An embargo proposed against a war enemy is forgotten twice as fast (30 turns on average) by that enemy compared with an embargo proposed against a non-hostile target (60 turns on average, as in BtS). [advc.130f]

Loading screen hint added about [advc.124] (naval blockade allows trade to pass through enemy tiles).

commit 525e919195916c8e061678b29cf21817eb9f07b8
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2018-11-03 12:35:02 +0100

Bugfixes in naval AI; reduced UWAI dogpiling; diplo decay tweaked
(DLL included)

Fixed a few potential issues with uses of CvUnit::canDefend. That function returns true for ships (except Work Boat), but often the AI actually needs to know whether a unit can defend against land units. [advc.001s]
One of these oversights (in K-Mod code) seems to have caused AI ships to ignore hostile units in AI_retreatToCity. I've written some new code that flags cities as safe or (potentially) unsafe at the start of an AI turn. Idle ships are never moved into unsafe cities; the AI keeps ships at sea if no city is safe. (I hope it works this way; not tested much yet.) [advc.139]

UWAI: Made the conditions for the "fair play" cost (quite a bit) less narrow. The AI had been dogpiling too recklessly in large-ish games.

Reduced the decay rate of AI diplo counters from 1.7% to 1.45% per turn. This corresponds to a half-life of about 50 turns (previously 40). [advc.130p,130m]

Deal cancelation during an AI turn is only immediately announced if it's an open borders agreement, defensive pact or peace treaty, or if a resource trade was canceled by the game rules (trade network severed); regular cancelation of gold and resource deals isn't announced until the start of the next human turn. [advc.106j]

CTD bug fixed in the previous commit. [advc.120f]

commit d7b78d09a226b9c9163076d616a3be788351f08e
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2018-10-30 14:14:01 +0100

Misc. changes to address issues that came up during a test game
(DLL included)

"You agreed to stop trading with our worst enemy" is remembered for only 50 turns on average (was 100). [advc.130f]

PerfectMongoose: Small adjustment of land-water ratio (less land) and river frequency (more rivers). [advc.021b]

Barbarian units may (probabilistic) heal instead of patrolling. [advc.306]

Made the AI a little less optimistic about stealing tiles with high foreign culture. [advc.031]

When a spy switches the civics or religion of a target civ, third parties that know the target are notified of the spy mission (without revealing the spy owner). And spy missions that switch a religion are recorded in replays. [advc.120f]

Only one decimal place is shown for revolt probabilities. [advc.023,advc.101]

The trade screen no longer lists resources on the human side that the AI already has (and doesn't need for a corporation either). (Such resources on the AI side had already been hidden.) [advc.004]

Rewrote the English and German help text for each difficulty and speed setting (shown on the final screen of the "Play Now" dialog chain) and for each map size and sea level setting (though these are actually unused as far as I can tell). [advc.137]
(Also replaced some spaces with tabs in Civ4GameText_advc.xml.)

commit cd20f1d77cd3f23c6206ecc7957543a631b8aa0f
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2018-10-25 12:24:16 +0200

Went through the 2nd half of the AdvCiv changelog in the manual and addressed some open issues
(DLL included)

Extended change [advc.001c] (GP birth probabilities in GP help text computed based on current GPP per turn) so that those probabilities are also shown directly on the GP bar and on the BUG GP progress bar (which is still disabled by default).

Change [advc.120c] (espionage slider hidden when at 0) can be disabled on the "General" tab of the BUG menu.

Got change [advc.001h] to work: The full city radius is highlighted when a Settler is selected and no yield icons are shown. The latter change is implemented through a bit of a hack: I've replaced CvUnit::isFound with a new function CvUnit::canFound, so that the EXE can't tell anymore whether a Settler is selected.

Tweaked [advc.120]: AI more likely to train Spies during war preparations (as in K-Mod), but not once war is declared.

Merged previously omitted parts of this K-Mod 1.45 change:
"The AI now counts feature defensive modifiers as having negative value
when adjacent to cities; and so they are more likely to chop forests
that are next to cities."
https://github.com/karadoc/Civ4-K-Mod/commit/f3819966a65ce8e04747aaf4d562a5fd18a0e5fe
However, this is only relevant if change [advc.012] is reverted; that change sets feature defense to 0 for Forest and Jungle and introduces a new XML tag "RivalDefense".

Changes [advc.121] (AI placement of Forts) and [advc.314] (promotions from goody huts partly based on surrounding terrain features) now work correctly with the RivalDefense ability of [advc.012].

Addition to [advc.250b]: No free wins against Barbarians if the R&F option is enabled.

[advc.300] no longer shows on the Settings tab on which turn Raging Barbarians can first appear because that info isn't really needed.

Barbarian Spearmen no longer stop appearing when the Barbarians reach the Medieval era. [advc.301]

Disabled the part of [advc.307] that had allowed Barbarian cities with local Ivory to train War Elephants.

Barbarian city placement exempted from changes to AI found value in the previous commit. [advc.108]
And some other minor changes to Barbarian found value. [advc.303]

When deciding whether to move the starting Settler, the AI assumes a low value for unexplored tiles. (Normally, 0 value is assumed for unexplored tiles.) [advc.108]

R&F: AI refuses to talk if its war utility is 20 or higher (threshold was 10 previously). [advc.705]

commit 6c16633c08f3bebb500ac6a571b50108a9a476a5
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2018-10-20 01:57:53 +0200

Mostly AI changes for the endgame; and AI moves starting Settler
(compiled DLL included)

UWAI: Improved code for thwarting imminent culture or space victory (practically hadn't been working at all). Also improved the logic for inter-continental war a little, and fixed a bug (in WarUtilityAspect::lostAssetScore) introduced through the recent performance improvements. Tweaked the military power modifiers from war success and personality.
Removed an obsolete hack for measuring distances between cities. [advc.104b]

The Conquest-4 AI victory strategy requires the AI to own at least 50% of the world's cities. During the endgame, civs with a very large total production can pursue a Space victory even if other civs are a bit ahead technologically. Both of these changes should make the AI more inclined to pursue a Space victory when there is a stalemate between two very powerful civs. [advc.115]

"Our mutual military struggle": When not currently sharing all wars, the relations bonus is reduced based on the duration and fierceness of the current wars instead of being set to 0. [advc.130m]

When an AI player is eliminated, the notification says that %PlayerName has been "defeated". (Previously said that the AI civ was "conquered", and originally that it was "destroyed".) [advc.099]

Merged/adapted from Better BUG AI: Code by Fuyu and Afforess that allows the AI to move its starting Settler. [advc.108]

Can again pillage improvements in neutral tiles containing rival units; change [advc.111] had prevented this. Can still not pillage routes in such tiles.

Went through the first half of the changelog in the manual and made some corrections.

Minor changes; mostly AI

The confirmation popup for a declaration of war lists all AI civs (if any) that are going to disapprove of the war. [advc.130h]

AttackOddsChangeRand from LeaderHead XML is only taken times 1.5 in the DLL (was times 2) when setting the attack bias for AI units. Since I don't want to make the Barbarians less inclined to attack, I've increased their AttackOddsChange values in XML. [advc.114d]

AI may guard a tile adjacent to a planned city site if that tile is better suited for fogbusting (terrain defense, visibility) than the planned city site. [advc.300]

Mostly non-functional changes
some from RoM and C2C

See my "update" under no. 1 here:
https://forums.civfanatics.com/threads/k-mod-bugs.637116/#post-15234855

The AI civics change algorithm can now handle negative civic values correctly. Not sure if these ever occur. [advc.131]

AI goes into multiple turns of anarchy willingly only if it has enough gold in the treasury to pay for gold deals during anarchy (to avoid strike). [advc.131]
Barbarians can no longer be affected by strikes. [advc.300]

Reduced military power value (AI heuristic) of Jaguar from 6 to 5 -- Montezuma is suicidal enough without overestimating his unique unit. [advc.104e]

Only relevant for the R&F option:
Merged a Kek-Mod change from April; but I'm only applying it to capitulated human vassals. [dlph.25]
"Human vassals are forced to vote for their master. Unless they are also a candidate themselves."
https://github.com/DarkLunaPhantom/Civ4-K-Mod/commit/4e4ccf707b78b020fd984412b2e9d0ea7c9eedd0

In Advanced Start, 1 city culture costs 1.5 start points (previously 1 start point, BtS 2.5) -- the first border expansion seemed a bit cheap. And the AI doesn't expand the borders of its capital unless it has a lot of points to spend. Inspired by this old "RoM: A New Dawn" revision by Afforess:
https://sourceforge.net/p/anewdawn/code/955/

AI tweaks merged from "RoM: A New Dawn" [advc.rom]
Not totally sure that they're needed in AdvCiv.
By Afforess:
"Count UNITAI_ATTACK units as city defenders when they are given a mission to guard a city"
https://sourceforge.net/p/anewdawn/code/756/

https://sourceforge.net/p/anewdawn/code/488/
By Koshling:
A condition for unloading Workers from ships was not quite correct
https://sourceforge.net/p/anewdawn/code/494/#diff-24
The AI values buildings that increase city's research rate more highly when the building-focus-research flag is set.
https://sourceforge.net/p/anewdawn/code/494/#diff-4

Non-functional changes:

Tag "RivalDefense" added to Civ4FeatureInfos.xml, and "Defense" gets back its BtS semantics, namely a defensive bonus for any unit in the tile, whereas RivalDefense only applies to units that belong to the same team as the tile owner.
This means, mod-mods can now revert change [advc.012] through XML.

Civ4lerts are no longer updated during AI Auto Play (to save CPU time). This was already the case for the alerts in MoreCiv4lerts.py and the alerts added by AdvCiv through the DLL; now all alerts are covered. [advc.127]

In game text, replaced the special characters é, à and ß before opening angle braces with escape sequences. Reportedly ...
https://forums.civfanatics.com/threads/accents-and-xml-errors-a-plea-to-all-modders.267556/
https://forums.civfanatics.com/threads/quick-modding-questions-thread.351519/page-266#post-15236624
... these character combinations can cause problems in the Japanese edition of Civ 4. I can't verify this, but those character combinations were easy enough to find and replace.

Added some const qualifiers to CvGame functions. [advc.003]

Removed a param added by [advc.127] from a DLLExport function (generally unsafe to add parameters to these).

Rearranged some UWAI code to improve performance in games with more than 18 civs. Now some 30% faster with 36 civs (but K-Mod is still more than twice as fast). No noticeable improvement with just 18 civs.

Added (but commented out in Civ4WorldInfos.xml) Giant map size for test games with more than 18 civs. From vincentz's 34-civs micro mod.
https://forums.civfanatics.com/resources/34-civ-gamecore-with-extra-map-sizes.23598/

Adopted from the We The People mod:

Made some classes non-copyable in order to make macros like GET_PLAYER less error-prone. [advc.003e]
Based on this Git commit by Nightinggale:
https://github.com/We-the-People-civ4col-mod/Mod/commit/f3d9441aef231b55a80aae66150c5829d27976c6

Inlined some functions (CvArea class) although I don't think this is going to accomplish much. [advc.003f]
Adopted from this commit by Swati-007:
https://github.com/We-the-People-civ4col-mod/Mod/commit/4916c23d6c79c6fc9495c4b93c7c53d8202f4d24
Related issue:
https://github.com/We-the-People-civ4col-mod/Mod/issues/106

commit 82ed992626a03f21b42345058cfdea526337641e
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2018-10-07 08:11:59 +0200

AI improvements, mostly related to naval war
(This commit contains some temporary code (marked with advc.tmp) that suppresses change advc.122, which is still work in progress.)

Minor (tentative) changes to the AI routines for picking up stranded units, and the game tries (but not very hard) to bump units to a landmass where their owner has a city. [advc.046]
Prompted by this CFC thread:
https://forums.civfanatics.com/threads/k-mod-bugs.637116/
Also fixed a bug in civics evaluation mentioned there, [advc.001]
and civics are now adopted probabilistically, as had already been the case for religions in AdvCiv. [advc.031]

Made AI less likely to raze the first city conquered on another continent if there is a good chance of conquering further cities on that continent. And some refactoring. [advc.116]

UWAI:
* Revised earlier changes to AI preparations for naval vs. land war.
* Increased impact of the victory-related WarUtilityAspects in the early game. In particular, stage 2 of the Conquest and Domination victory strategies can increase war utility now. (These victory stages had already made the K-Mod AI more warlike, but UWAI had so far only cared about stages 3 and 4.)
* Fixed an issue with AI civs dogpiling on a far stronger opponent when that opponent was already Furious at the dogpile attacker.
* Fixed a bug introduced in v0.93: Military power had been grossly underestimated when a defending civ had cities on several landmasses and a lot of Mounted or other fast units.
* Made the AI a little more optimistic about future uses for the military units that it expects to train for a war. Personality (BuildUnitProb) also matters a bit now. This change makes the AI a bit less worried about the cost of military build-up and a bit more worried about casualties.
* Fixed issues that had prevented the AI from starting a war by invading a vassal.  [advc.104j]

Debug mode: Exiting the Civics screen after switching to the perspective of another civ can no longer change the civics of the human civ. [advc.001]

Merged from the Dawn of Civilization mod:
"Minor civs cannot be involved as the other player in events" [advc.001]
https://github.com/dguenms/Dawn-of-Civilization/commit/d5313deda8960c63f3a1ab755961be64cb2f7e80

Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2018-10-06 13:55:03 +0200

Transport, East Indiaman changes
[advc.905b]

Transport: Reduced cost from 125 to 100, strength from 16 to 14, can't attack or pillage. Can't be built with Uranium.

East Indiaman: Unique Frigate with 3 cargo spaces and +1 speed. (Previously was a unique Galleon with +2 strength, +1 cargo space and the ability to explore rival territory.)
Increased the size of the 3D model.

Frigate, East Indiaman and Ship of the Line get +4 bombard rate.

Rationale: See this thread

and manual (.ODT) or Civilopedia.

commit 352a73a4b6ab44aa473e2d79f69f20174fb71b8d
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2018-10-05 09:34:04 +0200

Minor changes to AI war preparations and AI espionage

UWAI [advc.104s]:
* While preparing for a "total" war (as opposed to a limited war), the train-invader chance in cities is increased. (K-Mod only increases it once the war has been declared.)
* May, in rare cases, prepare for a naval assault even if a land path to the war target exists. If the Unit AI will then really deploy the units by ship remains to be seen.

Espionage:
* AI war plans no longer increase the odds of training a Spy. [advc.120]
* Espionage Economy strategy: Decreased AI espionage weights against civs from whom no techs can be stolen. [advc.120b]
* Debug mode: Switching the Espionage screen to the perspective of another civ no longer triggers failed assertions. [advc.001]

commit 20a48bbe1590699f79702bdf57c7059faf35cae6
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2018-09-30 16:06:46 +0200

Last batch of misc. changes (toward v0.94)
(barely tested so far)

Flood Plains are only placed orthogonally adjacent to rivers, not on river bends. [advc.129b]

Quechua loses its bonus against Archers and costs 15 (was 20 in K-Mod). [advc.907b]

Heal rate in cities under occupation reduced from 20 HP/turn to 15 (same as in friendly non-city tiles). [advc.023]

When the AI asks a player for help, it asks for the technology that it needs most instead of a random technology. [advc.144]

Revised AI trade value modifiers for technologies in Civ4TechInfos.xml. (These modifiers make the AI charge or pay 10% extra when trading certain technologies.) [advc.552]

"No Tech Brokering" no longer causes the AI to reject tech trades stating "We would have nothing to gain".
(Change 550e allows techs received in trade to be traded despite the no-brokering option if the recipient already had 67% progress when receiving the tech. Therefore, the nothing-to-gain answer is no longer needed.)

If the no-war probability from Civ4LeaderHeadInfos.xml would be 100 for a civ that the AI doesn't have Open Borders with, the AI computes its no-war probability as if its attitude toward the other civ was one level lower than it actually is. This should allow the AI to attack a human neighbor despite good relations if the human closes his or her borders so that the AI can't reach any other target. [advc.104y]

UWAI prediction of unit upgrades takes into account stockpiled gold (previously only based on GNP curve).

Cleaned up change advc.104i (This should make practically no functional difference.) MEMORY_STOPPED_TRADING_RECENT is no longer used for keeping track of recently declared wars; a new AI memory type MEMORY_DECLARED_WAR_RECENT is used instead. [advc.104i]

When two civs make peace, both become willing to talk to any remaining war parties. E.g. in the case of a triggered Defensive Pact, the two allies refuse to talk for some (randomized) amount of time in order to honor the Defensive Pact. If the first ally to become willing to talk made peace, previously, the other ally might have continued to refuse to talk. [advc.104i]

Some changes to random events that can start or end a war. These are just band-aids; a proper overhaul of random events would be a big task -- not worth it to me.
* When the AI gets the Great Mediator or Heroic Gesture event, it offers peace only with a 33% probability (was 50%). [advc.104z]
* Bug fixed in the BtS evaluation of AI responses to events: When a response causes a relations penalty, an unfavorable power ratio can make the BtS AI more inclined to pick this response; now power ratio has the opposite effect. This bugfix should make it unlikely that the AI provokes a catastrophic war through the Wedding Feud event. [advc.104z]
* When a war is ended through a random event, the replay log records this. (Wars started through an event had already been recorded in v0.93.) [advc.106g]

Expired peace treaties are canceled at the start of a round. Previously, they were canceled at the end of a turn, so a player who wanted to declare war right after the treaty expired had to cancel the peace deal manually. [advc.004]

Declarations of war on and by non-human vassals are never accompanied by the war-horn sound. And no sound when a non-human vassal makes peace.
If the Event Log is opened at the start of a turn (i.e. > 3 messages), declarations of war on or by a vassal and peace treaties with vassals are no longer shown on the main interface unless the active player is directly involved. [advc.106b]

Unit layer option "Non-military" renamed to "Civilians" (originally was "Domestics"). [advc.004z]

City name changes are recorded in replays. The game checks only at the end of each turn if the name has changed; i.e. changing a name multiple times or back and forth doesn't mess up the replay. [advc.106k]

Start and end of AI Auto Play are recorded in replays. [advc.127]

Rise & Fall option:
* Fixed a bug that had caused the AI to respond with "We can't accept gifts" even when trade was not restricted. [advc.705]
* Trade is restricted with civs that are ranked lower on the scoreboard than the player (previously: civs in the lower half); and the game era no longer matters. [advc.705]
* The start of an intermediate period between chapters is recorded in replays. [advc.706]

Some random refactoring. [advc.003]

Draft of future gameplay changes updated.

commit 5473a1906a6f5ba6b9daeddac3d3d1eafbe9bd74
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2018-09-29 01:09:55 +0200

Relations modifiers from trade further tweaked:
[advc.130p]
* Better adjusted to difficulty (modifiers were too high on medium and low difficulty settings), game speed and map size.
* Diminishing returns: It's harder to get from e.g. +3 to +4 "fair and forthright" trade than from 0 to +1.
* The AI subtracts 5/6 of the value that the other side receives instead of subtracting 100%. This means trades that are indeed "fair" contribute a bit to the fair-trade bonus.
* Now based on asset values at the time of the trade instead of game score.

Minor changes to AI asset values: Values of techs read from XML are reduced to 75%. [advc.131]

AI evaluation of extra trade routes from tech takes future cities into account. Currency is probably still underrated by the AI, but it's a step in the right direction. [advc.131]

commit 20d7ef5a94cf7377c1f6415d0370e3c54919037d
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2018-09-24 00:58:38 +0200

Rival-trade penalties, war trades on Glance tab, misc. changes:

Rival-trade memory ("you've traded with our worst enemy"): [advc.130p]
* Trades with a war enemy increase rival-trade memory even if that war enemy is not the worst enemy, though not as much as trades with the worst enemy do.
* Payments in peace deals increase rival-trade memory. Payments by a third party (brokered peace) are tolerated by civs that are Pleased with the civ with whom peace has been made. Payments in two-party peace deals count only partially for rival-trade memory.
* Annual trades (resources, gold per turn) are no longer double counted for trade memory during the first 10 turns.
* (Only relevant for mod-mods: The relations modifier is multiplied by MemoryAttitudePercent-EVENT_BAD_TO_US. This is set to 100% in Civ4LeaderHeadInfos.xml for every leader.)

The Glance tab shows a fist icon when a leader is willing to declare war on another leader. [advc.152]

Only one free AI Scout on Emperor difficulty (was 2). [advc.250e]

Hopefully fixed a problem with city names not appearing in announcements about diplo votes. [advc.150b]

PerfectMongoose: The Old World Starts option reserves the second largest continent for the New World only if this leaves enough room in the Old World; otherwise, smaller continents are reserved. [advc.021b]

Barbarians can no longer train or receive Cavalry, Anti-Tank and SAM Infantry. Musketman remains the only post-Medieval unit for Barbarians. The others had mattered too rarely and Barbarian MANPADS and bazookas were too jarring. [advc.307]

Minor changes in AI_unitValue to distinguish AI preferences for City Counter, City Special and Paradrop units more. [advc.005a]

... based on running AdvCiv and, for comparison, K-Mod 1.46 in the profiler. [advc.003b]

commit 836c1f898279224590e020d7880eb625b9c700ee

commit aa344656f656fda18d6175885be36707f857f263
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2018-09-11 02:14:05 +0200

AI changes, bugfixes, goody hut tweaks

I've realized that BtS didn't have any diplomatic handicap for human players (the penalty on Noble and above applied to human and AI civs alike), meaning that the bonus to inter-AI relations ("first impression") in AdvCiv is a novelty. Updated the v0.93 manual (start of DDiplo chapter) to reflect this. Going forward (v0.93b), I'm reducing the bonus to 0.3 on difficulty settings up to Noble. Above Noble, the bonus increases gradually from 0.4 (Prince) to 0.9 (Deity).

UWAI:
* Fixed problems in distance measurements between cities; AI had been underestimating long distances a lot. And an earlier performance tweak hadn't really been working; now that it is working, large maps play much faster. (250 turns of AI Auto Play on a Huge map with 18 civs have taken 17 minutes in a test, down from 24 minutes with v0.93.)
* Refined the computation of the opportunity cost of war; this should sometimes make the AI more willing to start war preparations during the Classical era.
* Tweaked the formula for weighing limited vs. total war from the previous commit.
* Military analysis distinguishes between MaxWarNearbyPowerRatio and LimitedWarPowerRatio from LeaderHead XML (had previously used the mean of the two for all non-naval wars). This change increases the impact of AI leader preferences for "limited" vs. "total" war.
* Slightly Increased the payment expected by an AI civ hired for war by another AI civ when hardly any fighting (or none) has taken place so far. Since v0.93, war allies had been brought in a bit too frequently for my taste.
* Bug fixed that had caused an AI civ hired for war to continue the war doggedly after the sponsor had made peace. Also, prior to Renaissance, the AI doesn't try to hire AI civs that have no land path to the target or only a very long land path.
* Bug fixed that had caused the AI to assume a too short preparation time after switching to a different target during war preparations.

The "fair and forthright" relations bonus is adjusted to the map size. Was previously too easy to get on Large and Huge maps. (This probably needs more work, but it's a start.) [advc.130p]

When the AI gets to choose a religion ("Choose Religions" game option), and its favorite isn't available (or it doesn't have a favorite), the AI picks the one with the cheapest tech requirement. (BtS chooses uniformly at random in this case.) [advc.171]

Reduced impact of Growth AI flavor on city placement (Found value). The first few AI cities had been placed a bit too far apart sometimes. [advc.031]

Goody hut tweaks:
* Increased the probability of free tech at the expense of free gold. (Tech is more interesting than gold and no longer overpowered.) Gold is still a bit likelier than tech. [advc.314]
* Increased the expected amount of research from 50 to 55 (on Normal speed) and increased the peak turn from 225 to 250. [advc.314]
* A Scout (or Explorer) entering a hut can't generate another Scout if its owner already has two or more exploration units. [advc.315d]
* On Epic and Marathon speed: Reduced the grace period in the early game during which no Warrior or free XP can be received. [advc.314]

Barbarian cities flip on the second revolt. (Previously, as in BtS, on the first revolt. Too easy.) [advc.101]

Moved the Resource Layer button so that it's shown at the same position in Globe view as in non-Globe view. And swapped the Bare Map and Show Yields buttons. [advc.004z]

Text for button to toggle gridlines says "Toggle Grid" instead of "Toggle Tiles". [advc.005g]

Fixed typos in theme files that had lead to confusing log output. [advc.002b]

For mod-mods: The "fair and forthright" relations bonus is multiplied by MemoryAttitudePercent-EVENT_GOOD_TO_US in Civ4LeaderHeadInfos.xml. That percentage is 100 for all leaders, but could be modified to make some leaders easier to please through trade than others. [advc.130p]

Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2018-08-31 22:57:50 +0200

Various minor changes

(Not tested much; no DLL committed)

Bug fixed in advc.315a: Scouts had been able to conquer empty cities.

UN and AP don't require Diplo victory to be enabled, but the victory votes do. [advc.178]

Settings tab (Victory screen) shows which victory conditions are disabled. [advc.004]

Removed an AdvCiv DLL change that had halved the culture garrison strength (CGS) of Mechanized units. The CGS of Mechanized Siege units is now halved through XML instead, and Armored units have the same CGS as in BtS. The reduced CGS for tanks wasn't necessarily realistic, and restoring the BtS value allows me to remove a sentence from the main portion of the manual. [advc.101]

UWAI: Increased the impact of AI leader preferences for limited vs. total war.

Reduced the trade value counted for "fair and forthright" trade a bit when trading technologies. [advc.551]

Reduced the impact of military power on the trade value of technologies. [advc.550a]

Increased the expected time that the AI remembers a razed city from 60 to 75 turns, and from 70 to 75 turns for "you brought in a war ally". The latter is now forgotten exactly twice as fast as "you declared war on us" (150 turns) and the former twice as fast as "you razed a holy city" (also 150 turns). [advc.130r]

Tweaked unit AI types (Civ4UnitInfos.xml): Swordsman and Legionary (a.k.a. Praetorian) are City Attackers by default and Battleship gains the (non-default) AI type Escort. [advc.131]

Reduced the impact of personality-based weights on unit types trained by AI cities, increased the impact of personality-based weights for improvements built by the AI (though the impact is still small), and reassigned most of the weights for greater historical accuracy (though the weights still don't work well in that regard) and to avoid boosting unique units too much. [advc.005a]

Made Hinduism Suryavarman's favorite religion because he was a Hindu, not a Buddhist. [advc.005a]

Corrected some info on the "Diplomacy" page in Civilopedia (under "Game Concepts"). [advc.008,advc.130o,advc.104i]

commit a133dd74a0ca49932a3e167cc5fc998c9a33eeb6
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2018-08-20 09:09:58 +0200

Changed the way DeclareWarTradeRand (DWTR) is used by the AI.

This value from LeaderHead XML determines the probability of checking for war trades. The probability is now 10 / (DWTR + 10 * min{10,t}), where t is the number of turns that the war has lasted. For small t, the resulting probability is lower than in BtS, and, for large t, it's higher. I might set an individual DWTR value for each leader at a later point; currently they all have DWTR=40 as in BtS. [advc.161]

Revised code for change advc.104i (UWAI willing to talk only according to BtS rules when fighting a war for a third party). Now also applies when an AI civ is paid to start a war, which is what the manual had been saying all along.

commit 69b231572a2f96ffd55352425830a23d7f3fca1d
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2018-08-18 13:44:11 +0200

Bugs fixed in AI war trade behavior; tech cost adjustments

Fixed two bugs introduced in the commit from July 31 that had prevented most – if not all – war trades (hired war) between AI civs.

Reduced the price charged by the AI for an embargo by
* removing some double counting from my formula [advc.130f] and
* fixing a vanilla Civ 4 bug that was causing all AI trades to be added to the price, not just trades with the target of the embargo. [advc.001q]
Due to advc.104o, the embargo price also serves as a lower bound for the price for war trades, so these changes also make hired war cheaper.

Increased the cost of an embargo against a militarily powerful civ when the embargo is not part of a war trade. [advc.130f]

"You stopped trading with us" is forgotten after 60 turns on average (same as "You negotiated a trade embargo"). I had missed this one when I set decay rates for all AI memory. [advc.130r]

The extra cost for war trades charged by UWAI to allay suspicions about ulterior motives of the sponsor no longer applies to AI sponsors in most cases, and I've reduced the cost in any case.

Bug fixed in change advc.130p: The AI's worst enemy was sometimes not updated correctly.

Further revised the values in Civ4WorldInfo.xml: [advc.140]
* Can draft 1 fewer unit per turn on Large and Huge maps.
* 1 fewer free bonus from the national wonders Broadway, Rock'n'Roll and Hollywood on Standard-size, Large and Huge maps.
* War weariness increased on Large (now 87%, previously 70%) and Huge maps (now 75%, previously 50%).
* Minor adjustments to the map-size modifiers for trade route profit, tech costs and distance maintenance.

When Civilopedia is accessed from the main menu, the map-size-based number of draftable units and free bonuses (see above) are shown as a range; e.g. "Nationhood: Can draft 2-4 units per turn". [advc.004y]

Adjusted tech costs to match the changes to AI and rules between v0.92 and 0.93:
* Increased tech costs in the Classical and Medieval era. [advc.910}
* Reduced tech costs on Noble difficulty. [advc.251]

As a temporary measure, assigned negative AI weights to Feudalism and Iron Working because the AI had been prioritizing these techs too much. [advc.131b]

DLL and manual (ODT) included, but still being tested.

Some small AI changes

A minor tweak to the probability of AI tech trades. [advc.550]

UWAI:
* Minor changes to the AI simulations of early mounted attacks that might make the AI more willing to attack across a longer distance with Horse Archers
* On a similar note, I've increased the impact of MaxWarMinAdjacentLandPercent (MWMALP). This AI personality parameter had previously only affected the UWAI evaluation of target cities that might be difficult to hold onto. Now also affects the deployment distances assumed in the military analysis. (In BtS, MWMALP all but rules out war against non-adjacent targets for some leaders; it's still not nearly as significant in AdvCiv.)
* Lowered the payment expected by the AI for starting a war by about 10% and reduced the random component of the calculation.
* Reduced the impact of the potential for unit upgrades on the military build-up predicted by the AI.

commit fb302b8082477c5b59b987f5c1b83d5bd5ad1b84
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2018-07-08 19:45:11 +0200

Various AI tweaks

UWAI:
* Possibly improved UWAI behavior for starting multiple wars. The AI should now be more likely to end an ongoing war (shortly) before starting a different one.
* Made the AI less optimistic about getting war enemies to capitulate.
* Fixed minor issues with the counting of lost units in military simulations and making peace when war utility is near 0 (i.e. when the AI isn't sure whether to make peace).

Diplo values:
* The AI counting of memory values (e.g. "years of peace") is now adjusted to the game speed setting. (Based on the Growth modifier in the Ancient era, based on the Golden Age modifier in the Medieval era and later, and based on the mean of Growth and Golden Age in the Classical era.) Especially early on, too many relations bonuses had been accumulating (peace, open borders). [advc.130k]
* Fixed a bug in my rounding function: negative values had not been rounded to the closest integer. This had resulted in higher relations modifiers for scoreboard rank and "first impressions" than intended. [advc.003]
* Rewrote the formula for border tensions, increasing the importance of stolen and unworkable tiles. [advc.147]

Tech value:
* AI tech value for Animal Husbandry and Agriculture increased relative to Bronze Working and Iron Working. This was necessary because the AI is now (since v0.92) aware that health isn't important in the early game. Also had to water down the changes to chop value from K-Mod 1.46 a bit. [advc.036,k146]
* Tech value of units: AI doesn't magically know whether it is geographically isolated until turn 30. [advc.131]
* Reduced the tech value counted for buildings and trade routes in the very early game in order to make the AI less likely to research Sailing very early on. [advc.131]
* Decreased the random value added to AI tech value (had too much impact in the early game). [advc.131]

AI found value for river tiles tweaked; Tundra rivers had been overrated. [advc.031,advc.053]

Delayed the appearance of Barbarian cities on small, unclaimed landmasses because it's difficult for the AI to conquer such cities. [advc.300]

Non-AI changes:
* Aligned the color coding of relations values in change advc.004q with the colors on the Glance tab. (Green for Pleased and Friendly, Red for Annoyed and Furious)
* Sevopedia lists only improvements that can be built, and not e.g. City Ruins or "Land Worked". "Barbarian" no longer listed under leaders. [advc.004y]
* Fixed a minor issue with city and tile culture conversion in my implementation of change dlph.23.

commit 1fb46dad5aae05c5b953bab6b6106440c08b2676
Author: f1rpo <hidden>

commit c6aea2ef244d56ab2f5318231077cf3116d08f26
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2018-07-01 23:51:46 +0200

Revised AI_foundValue
(the function that determines where the AI settles)
Still hardly tested
[change id: advc.031 if none stated]

Fixed a bug I introduced in v0.90; the bug had caused K-Mod code to be ignored that discourages settling on top of a resource. While looking for the bug, I've improved said K-Mod code a bit (hopefully). Also fixed a related issue introduced around that time: when a resource could not yet be improved, no bonus yields had been counted for that resource. Now bonus yields are counted if the tech requirement for trading the resource is currently researchable.

Fixed a bug in K-Mod; code that was supposed to reduce found values in the proximity of other civs had had no effect. [advc.001]

Per-tile utility values are now sorted in descending order and summed up with decreasing weights, meaning that more weight is given to tiles that are likely to be worked early in the city's life. (Previously, I had achieved a similar effect by taking per-tile values to the power of 1.25, but this would also encourage cities with uniformly good tiles, e.g. 20 river plains, when it might be better to split that area between several cities.)

Some utility is counted for tiles in the radii of other cities, i.e. tiles that could be stolen from rival cities or shared with a friendly city.

Rearranged code to make the function easier to read and to avoid some unnecessary or counterproductive computations. (It's still messy though, both conceptually and in terms of implementation.)

Reconciled my own code for discouraging low-food sites and sites with too many bonus resources (advc.052) with BtS code serving a similar purpose.

Reduced the AI aversion for sites with numerous bad tiles (e.g. Tundra) a bit further.

In scenarios, the choice of the best city sites (function AI_updateCitySites) and the site that Settlers move to are now slightly randomized so that the AI doesn't necessarily pick the same spots for its first couple of cities in the same order every game. [advc.052]

Unrelated to found value:
The AI is now less likely to build Barracks before its first Settler. [advc.017]
Animals can't appear on Desert and Snow tiles unless there is freshwater. [advc.300]

commit 7ea56f5bff2418f04df2c9f957dca7e47712e209
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2018-06-22 10:55:37 +0200

Some changes for scenarios:

In scenarios, civs receive free tech from the game difficulty in addition to any free techs defined by the scenario. (E.g. AI civs start with Archery and Archers when playing the World18Civs scenario on Monarch difficulty, whereas, in BtS, they only start with Warriors.) [advc.051]

The rule changes about peaks separating continents and ice separating oceans (advc.030) now apply to scenarios like they were supposed to. (But still don't apply to complex scenarios with preplaced cities like Earth1000AD.) [advc.051]

Changes to AI found value:
* On maps/ scenarios with densely placed resources, the AI avoids claiming too many resources with a single city. [advc.052]
* "Greed" adjustment (based on AI personality and traits) removed because K-Mod's adjustment for Creative leaders does largely the same thing. [advc.031]
* AI encouraged to settle in tiles that connect two large bodies of water. Only works if the water bodies are separated by land or ice though. [advc.031]

commit 7e2049e85cbff9227b4148c567d0e37b261c3381
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2018-05-31 11:05:24 +0200

Merged changes by DarkLunaPhantom (Kek-Mod 0.20) from late May.
https://github.com/DarkLunaPhantom/Civ4-K-Mod/commits/master

When a city is traded or gifted (regardless of whether it's liberated), 33% of the old owner's city culture is converted into city culture of the new owner. Change advc.122 was already converting culture in tiles surrounding the city, but I guess I forgot to also convert city culture. The new owner only receives free units when the city is liberated during the creation of a colonial vassal. (BtS creates free units when a city is given to any kind of vassal at any time.) [dlph.23]

"Colonies don't inherit espionage points from parent civ anymore." [dlph.24]

"Reworked resolutions. Fixed multiple bugs and inconsistencies with conditions for proposing and defying resolutions. War resolution can be proposed against voting members (and non-members). Defensive pacts with attackers are cancelled before implementing war resolution. Players can defy resolution assigning them a city. AI can choose (and vote) to repeal resolutions." [dlph.25]

Capitulated vassals can't defy any resolutions; voluntary vassals can't defy resolutions on war and peace. Can vote to implement a DP when all members share all wars. [dlph.25]
(This is a bit different from what Kek-Mod does.)

And a couple of unrelated changes:
* Messages generated when applying a random event are excluded from the replay log. Was supposed to work this way since v.8x(?). [advc.106g]
* Removed some unused functions from a UWAI class.

Revised GP Research info and moved it into the footer of the Tech Advisor like in RFC: Dawn of Civilization. GP Research is still disabled by default. [advc.004a]

Stealth Destroyer can detect submarines. Adopted from RFC: Dawn of Civilization. [advc.906]

commit 6b49d868b4cf1e3627136d826b1bc83d5756856a
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2018-05-20 22:15:20 +0200

Multiplayer debugging and a couple of other changes

Replaced most calls to getChtLvl (set by the EXE to 0 in multiplayer) with isDebugMode, so that Shift, Ctrl and Alt can be used to access extra help text when debugging multiplayer games. [advc.135c]
Disabled a few lines of Debug text in multiplayer because they require calls to CvPlot::getFoundValue, a function that caches its return value, which is generally unsafe in multiplayer. [advc.001n]

In multiplayer, AI Auto Play stops when the game goes out of sync. Can use AI Auto Play when playing with Simultaneous Turns. [advc.127]

Added an XML switch for creating multiple MPLog files when playing a Direct IP test game on a single PC. [advc.007]

Fixed OOS errors in AdvCiv code, all related to misuses of CvGame::getActivePlayer.
* Replaced calls to getCenterUnit with plotCheck. [advc.315, advc.300]
* UWAI no longer uses getActivePlayer to determine whose turn it is.
* PerfectMongoose no longer calls the active player's found-value function. [advc.021b]

Largely rewrote the code for assigning starting locations based on difficulty (CvGame::assignStartingPlots): [advc.108b]
* StartingLocPercent from the difficulty setting now applies in multiplayer games, i.e. human civs no longer get the best starting locations (unless playing on Settler). [advc.108b]
* Made CvGame::assignStartingPlots compatible with PerfectMongoose (which previously ignored difficulty). [advc.021b]

Set StartingLocPercent for Noble difficulty to 49% so that humans get average starts when there is an odd number of players (was 40%, i.e. better than average). Also increased StartingLocPercent slightly (by 5 percentage points) for most of the other difficulty settings. [advc.108b]

Fixed a bug in advc.021b: The PerfectMongoose map had failed to find starting locations in games with just 2 or 3 civs.

"Domestic" option of the Unit layer renamed to "Non-Military" because the option highlights all tiles with visible non-combat units; it's not restricted to tiles owned by the active player. The indicator bubbles now always show a non-military unit (instead of the top defender). [advc.004z]

Major events are purged from the Event Log after 100 turns (kept permanently in BtS). [advc.106b]

"Play a scenario" no longer grayed out on the main menu. [advc.002g]

commit 6af01f82d3296666a2a16ef16ca77b559040231d
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2018-05-19 09:50:11 +0200

Moved developer settings from GlobalDefines_advc.xml into a new file GlobalDefines_devel.xml. [advc.009]

XML switches for enabling AI Auto Play, WorldBuilder and Debug mode in multiplayer games; disabled by default. See GlobalDefines_devel.xml for details. [advc.127, advc.135c]
I still haven't done much multiplayer testing, but at least I have the necessary tools now.

Updated manual included (only ODT).

commit 54657a47236c070268ed0be9958f552a6ec3a8a6
Author: f1rpo <hidden>

Minor misc. changes

When a vassal is technologically far ahead of its master and doesn't dislike the master, the vassal AI may decide to gift tech to the master. [advc.112]

The game may kill Barbarian units at random when too many accumulate in a city that doesn't share a continent with any civ, "too many" meaning more than 1.5 times the city population. Earlier AdvCiv code was already killing units when a continent became too crowded. [advc.300]

Announcements about completed wonders no longer say e.g. "the Stonehenge" (just "Stonehenge"). [advc.008e]

The Culture layer shows the tile owner's color even if that civ has less than 20% relative tile culture (a threshold added by K-Mod). [advc.004z]

Changes to announcements, mostly for AI Auto Play

During AI Auto Play, the color of announcements and their map locations are no longer discarded; i.e. one can now inspect the location of an announcement by clicking on it in the Event Log once Auto Play has ended. Added location info to numerous announcements so that the camera moves somewhere when the announcement is clicked on in the Event Log. E.g. clicking on a declaration of war now moves the camera to the capital of the civ that declared war (if that capital is revealed). Added a loading screen hint about clicking on stuff in the Event Log. [advc.127b]

Canceled Defensive Pacts are announced and recorded in replays. [advc.106f]

Upon closing, Military Advisor forgets which players were selected.

commit efd81e66a2a45883179fe685686deda828de4b09
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2018-05-05 00:21:17 +0200

Changes to Privateer
[advc.033]
Rules change: Civs in a vassal-master relationship can't use Privateers to pillage each other's improvements, blockade each other's cities nor to block tiles from being worked (CvUnit::canSiege). Combat still allowed.

AI changes:
* When selecting a city to plunder, attitude and the city owner's tech are taken into account. Depending on attitude and DeclareWarThemRefuseAttitudeThreshold, the AI doesn't use Privateers against ships and improvements.
* Capitulated vassals don't train Privateers (unless they somehow don't have any better general-purpose ship).
* Cities stop training Privateers once half of the world has entered the Industrial era.

In Debug mode, Privateer ownership is revealed and Privateers are shown on the Military Advisor. [advc.007]

(for the third time). "Investigate City" had never been shown in green. [advc.120d]

commit a939fbeefb83e987e90c08f4bd64b7515a1738dc
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2018-05-02 12:12:06 +0200

Cosmetic changes concerning Civilopedia and the Apostolic Palace:

Removed garbled text from Civilopedia pages for Projects. I thought I had fixed this in v0.92, but I had in fact only fixed it for Buildings.

Changed the names of wonders so that they don't start with "The". E.g. just "Taj Mahal" instead of "The Taj Mahal". This was highly inconsistent and made it harder to find wonders on the Wonders tab (Info screen) and in Civilopedia. [advc.008e]

Yield bonuses to religious buildings (defined in Civ4VoteSourceInfos.xml) are displayed in help text and Civilopedia text about the vote source building. I.e. the production bonus from Apostolic Palace is no longer kept secret. [advc.179]

Changed the start era restriction of the Great Wall, Statue of Zeus (now both available when starting in the Classical era) and the Shwedagon Paya (now unavailable when starting in Renaissance). [advc.008a, advc.310]

Civilopedia only shows the start era restrictions of wonders (e.g. "Stonehenge can only be built on Ancient era and earlier starts") when the restriction affects the wonder in the current game (i.e. no restrictions shown in games that start in the Ancient era). When accessing the Civilopedia from the main menu, the start era restriction is only shown if the start era threshold is unusual -- most wonders become unavailable when starting two eras later than the wonder's tech era, and this isn't spelled out anymore. [advc.008a]

Civilopedia only shows victory conditions required for buildings when accessed from the main menu or when the respective victory condition is disabled in the current game. [advc.008a]

Added some info about AI voting behavior to the Civilopedia article about the UN victory. [advc.148]

Shortened "Beyond the Sword" to "BtS" in Civilopedia page names. [advc.002g]

commit ce3cf805f64807a437e192e40e62dc5064c4f41c
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2018-04-20 09:10:08 +0200

Further adjustments to the goody hut changes. In particular:
- Increased peak turn from 200 to 225.
- Huts can no longer grant progress toward Industrial and Modern tech.
- Hostile units can have mixed types, e.g. 1 Archer and 1 Warrior.
- Free units can start with up to two free promotions.
- Spy can't get a free unit from a guarded hut (b/c it would land on top of the Barbarian unit guarding the hut).
- AI units on "Reserve" enter a Tribal Village if one happens to be near them. This can matter when the AI founds a colony on an unexplored landmass.
[advc.314]

Anti-Barbarian combat bonuses from the difficulty setting are now shown as "from Difficulty Level" in help text. (Showing e.g. "+115% vs. Barbarians" for Scout was a bit confusing.) [advc.315c]

Cleaned up some redundant code in CvGameTextMgr used by both the Advanced Combat Odds mod (ACO) and the base game and fixed two minor bugs in ACO code.

When choosing a tech, the AI applies flavor values multiplicatively rather than additively. [advc.020]
Tbd.: Set more sensible flavor values in XML (TechInfos).

Removed an outdated loading screen hint. [advc.130j]

commit bb7b6925d1666957c0cc1239e8e8df7c63a5df09
Author: f1rpo <hidden>

Small misc. changes; no DLL committed for now.

The default field-of-view setting now takes the aspect ratio into account, not just the horizontal resolution. The FIELD_OF_VIEW in GlobalDefines.xml is ignored. [advc.004m]

The Event Log no longer opens automatically when playing without "Minimize Pop-Ups". There's an XML switch to override this. [advc.106b]

Enabled the "willing to talk" alert by default and disabled the "peace treaty" alert by default. (Only affects new installations of AdvCiv.) [advc.106d]

Increased AI-to-human contact delay for Open Borders on Epic and Marathon speed. [advc.124]

UWAI:
* Tweaked the impact of war success.
* AI not willing to talk when it would demand more than 1/6 of the human player's cities, rounded up, for peace.
* Minor bug fixed that had caused the AI to predict the military build-up of human civs as either "full" or "normal", but never as "increased".
* Added a clause for handling national unit limits to the code that predicts military build-up. (only relevant for mod-mods)

Removed the BUG help files in French and Spanish - turned out these were just copies of the English help file, and it seems that no French or Spanish translation exists. This shaves 1 MB off AdvCiv's download size. [advc.009]

Adopted a change from History Rewritten v1.25: "City artstyle now determined by highest culture present," but disabled it again because there appears to be no way to do the same for Cottages, Hamlets etc. XML switch: ENABLE_005F in GlobalDefines_advc.xml.

Cottages etc. no longer grow during disorder. [advc.001]

Resource trade:
* Extended the resource trades alert so that it triggers also when the AI becomes willing to import one of the player's surplus resources. [advc.210e]
* Some minor changes to make the AI less willing to import Coal prior to Railroad and Steam Power. [advc.036]
* Fixed (hopefully) a potential OOS issue with with advc.036 (AI resource evaluation) in multiplayer.

UWAI:
* Fixed a bug introduced in v0.85 that had caused the AI to grant gift requests very rarely.
* Fixed an issue with the AI becoming willing to talk, but agreeing to no peace deal. This had occurred when the human player embargoed a war enemy.
* Fixed a bug (since v0.80) in the war utility computation that had caused AI civs to ignore losses incurred by their vassals.
* Reduced the cost assigned by UWAI for losing a trade partner when declaring war on a human civ. (Rationale: humans are often willing to reconcile after being attacked.) Was supposed to work this way from the beginning, but I had implemented only one half (AI more willing to make peace with a human in hopes of reconciliation).
* (A few comments added about minor known issues.)

(Commented out in the code because it seems like a needless complication on second thought and dubious historically: When a city is clearly about to be conquered, the AI is willing to cede that city in a peace deal.)  [advc.122]

commit 2989cda6e0a97c60bd15d38a45913209af8c701e
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2018-02-22 16:00:07 +0100

Toward v0.92 (no DLL committed):

Improvements to the PerfectMongoose (PM) map script:
* The break-Pangaea code is now properly disabled when playing without the Old World Starts option. It was supposed to work this way since v0.89.
* Further reduced the number and size of meteors used for breaking Pangaea (when playing with Old World Starts).
* Smoothed the impact of meteors on elevation levels; fewer Peaks along coasts this way and more Peaks elsewhere.
* Increased the size of the Old World a bit. Continents that are too small for civs to start on are now always counted as part of the New World.
* Let PM assign starting locations even when playing without Old World Starts. Previously, I had used the standard assignment algorithm from the DLL instead.
* Made the PM assignment of starting locations prioritize distance between starting locations more.
* Fur no longer appears on high elevations in low latitudes.

Tidied up some changes from the last few commits; also some bugfixes.

Usability improvements for AI resource trades:
* When an AI civ both buys resources from and sells resources to another AI civ, the AI tries to cancel those deals and replace them with resource-for-resource trades. This should make the AI trades easier to read on the Foreign Advisor. [advc.036]
* In one-for-one resource trades, the AI is willing to give human players a small discount in order to avoid asking for 1 gold per turn in addition. [advc.036]
* AI willingness to cancel resource deals due to uneven trade values now based on the age of the deal. [advc.133]

Merged a bugfix by EmperorFool: Accessing the Civilopedia from the main menu (without having started or loaded a game first) no longer causes Sevopedia to become permanently disabled. [advc.004y]

Went through the post-K-Mod BUG commit history and merged another bug fix: The strategy overlay (dot map) no longer disappears after loading a savegame.

Replays record the first discovery of each tech. Can alternatively (XML switch) report when a civ enters a new era. Replay messages about founded cities are shown in the city owner's text color. [advc.106]

Decreased the value assigned by the City AI to Moai Statues and Globe Theater in the capital. [advc.131]

Bug fixed in advc.004b (projected expenses shown on city found button): Had not been taking into account colony maintenance in other cities.

Barbarians are no longer able to train Paratroopers. [advc.307]

PublicMaps folder changed to "PrivateMaps" based on TC01's recommendation here:
https://forums.civfanatics.com/threads/private-and-public-maps.355021/

commit ed1826faf199f4a18442c9196f181a46797e79c1
Author: f1rpo <hidden>
Date:   2018-02-18 11:27:13 +0100

Toward v0.92 (no DLL committed):
Adjustments to advc.099b (tile culture decay in city radii):
* Tiles contested between more than two civs are now handled sensibly. Removed the term "exclusive radius" from the manual and some variable names because it's misleading: decay can also apply to a tile within the radius of more than one city.
* Tiles owned by Barbarians and city tiles exempted from decay beyond the 1% that applies to all tiles.
* Decay rate reduced a little.

Two bugs fixed in k146 (tech evaluation): illegal tech selected; infinite loop.
Made the AI less interested in beelining toward powerful techs (DepthRate reduced from 0.8 to 0.6). [k146]

UWAI:
* Improved the performance of distance measurements. This could speed up AI turns in the late game by some 5%.
* Improved the simulation of wars between civs spread across multiple landmasses. That said, there's still hardly any AI warfare on Archipelago before the Industrial era. Conquering small islands is just rarely worth the effort because the tactical AI needs a lot of units to pull it off (and then faces problems with stranded units).
* Fixed an issue with war utility from "bellicosity" counting war successes of allies.
Plus some misc. tweaks, mostly for performance.

UI (minor):
* Fixed some bugs that I had introduced in v0.90 when refactoring CvGameTextMgr.cpp. Help text for buildings hadn't been shown in Civilopedia when viewing it from the main menu, and some info was missing (e.g. the project names in the help text for the projects list).
* Added unit costs to the help text for the units list (were missing in BtS).
The number of buildings required for national wonders is now computed assuming Standard world size when accessing Civilopedia from the main menu (instead of Duel size). [advc.004y]
* Get to see all foreign units in Military Advisor when in Debug mode. (Wish I had done that long ago. And how easy it was!) Can no longer switch some of the Advisor screens to the Barbarian perspective when in Debug mode. [advc.007]
